Review: Planned on using this for Freedompop. It would not activate on freedompop.Make sure you get the ESN and confirm it with a phone provider before buying one of these. Even though this is an SPH-L720 and I had another SPH-L720 on freedompop already -- there are ESN based restrictions placed by the carrier. Sprint only releases a certain range of ESN numbers to freedompop. The SPH-L720 that I happened to receive was restricted to JUST Sprint, and I could not use freedompop with it.Ended up switching to Sprint anyway, so I am keeping the phone. Sprint happeend to have a good deal on unlimited talk/text/data. So it worked out.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 16GB SPH-720T LTE Tri-Band Blue (Sprint)
Review: This phone is advertised as a Sprint phone. Don't know the full history, but it was unlocked at some point by software called Chameleon to make it usable on other networks. Under settings/About Device/Build ID, it has characters in Arabic, showing that the phone was unlocked. Per Sprint representative, that means the phone can only be used as a voice/text phone, and data via wifi, but never data via Sprint network--data via network is whole purpose of a Smartphone!!!Thankfully has 30 day return and will be returning this shortly. Do not buy this phone if you intend to use on a Sprint network, even though it is advertised as Sprint, it is not compatible!!!One great plus, Amazon and company apparently have a policy to allow return with free return shipping to boot!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Active Dive I9295 Unlocked International Version Blue
Review: It was love at first read. After I looked at the specs when I saw it being advertised at my local carrier's store I had to get my hands on one. So I did, and a two-year contract to boot, but then my phone was stolen last month so I had to buy a new one, exact same color (dive blue) exact same model (international i9295). The phone came brand new and in a timely manner and even came with a bunch of things that didn't come with the original phone that my carrier store gave me, like cool ear buds and an international adapter. I just used the charging cord and adapter that I had with my old phone, so the bulk of the charger in this new phone doesn't bother me, I just stashed it somewhere.The phone is a bit heavy, but nothing I couldn't get used to. It actually helps to remind me that I don't have it like when I pick my purse up and it isn't heavy enough I look around until I find my phone. Also, yes the phone is a little large and almost ridiculously so if you've never been on the "big screen" bandwagon but TRUST ME YOU WILL HOP ON THAT BANDWAGON FASTER THAN A HEAT FAN WHO KNOWS NOTHING ABOUT BASKETBALL AND SWITCHED TO THE SPURS THIS YEAR. Yep, pretty quick.The battery life isn't the best, but after looking through lifehacker I saw an app called Greenify which came highly recommended and I installed it and never looked back. The time between charges even for a social media lover like me improved markedly. However I don't ever play games on my phone so I think that also helps, I'm not that kind of person.The color, oh the color. My favorite color in the world just happened to be the color of this phone. Sigh. Could it be any more dreamy?The processing speed was never an issue. I had things like YouTube, social media apps, GMail, my university's website, etc. open and I never saw it freeze. Keep in mind I had this phone for 6 months. Installed/uninstalled many a failed app that didn't live up to my standards. But I keep my phone clean and virus-free so that might be a factor.Get the phone already what are you waiting for?? Buy a medium-hard protective rubbery case for it and drop it in the sink accidentally while washing dishes, trust me it will pay for itself with the water resistant feature. KEEP IN MIND IT SAYS WATER RESISTANT, NOT WATERPROOF. WHOEVER ON HERE SAYS "WATERPROOF" & "DID NOT WORK" ANYWHERE IN THEIR REVIEW SHOULD NOT BE TRUSTED. It is only water resistant IF you seal the phone properly beforehand, since my phone was kept constantly sealed by my tight case, I never had a problem dropping it various times into shallow water.Sure, you could have a bigger better phone with bigger faster processing speed and a more updated CPU but if you were gonna get one you wouldn't be reading my review so closely, now would you?

Since I really did want the phone (it was purchased as a gift for my son), I was willing to pay to have it fixed, and in fact made several attempts to find a local place to repair it. NONE of the places I checked with, including Radio Shack, would fix it. Some of them said they would fix other Samsung phones, but not the "Active" model. At first the seller tried to say that the description on Amazon said that the phone was cracked, and that it was "as is" and could not be returned. Review: I'm deployed to Afghanistan, and was worried about the dust. I knew I wanted an Android phone, so I did my research. After learning that Samsung made a water/dust proof Galaxy phone, I figured I had to give it a try (as I wanted an S4, and thought this was the best of both worlds). I was a little nervous about the screen, as this phone has a TFT LCD (where as the Flagship S4 has an OLED screen), but so far I haven't had any issues. In fact, as I was researching this phone to make sure it was the best choice for my needs, I read that the TFT LCDs are actually better for outside use (as they don't get as washed out by the sun - something I was concerned about), and so far I haven't had any issues viewing the screen outdoors (even in direct sunlight).I would absolutely recommend this phone for anyone who spends a lot of time outdoors, but still want's a smartphone. My only complaint about this phone is that I can't use a lot of the really cool add-on's/accessories that are available for the S4 (such as Qi wireless charging, or the flip cover that shows the time through a window on the front, etc...) Other than that minor qualm, I've been extremely satisfied with this phone.P.S. - Yes, I've tested the "waterproof-ness" of this phone, and it works like a champion - just make sure you do what the directions say and verify that the back is completely on (being mindful of the clip/latch under the camera on the back).

The product came on time and worked fine for 5 days then the display stopped working even though the screen did't show any crack. The phone would still turn on but the screen was completely black and I could hear some background noise. We took it to the Samsung shop and they said they could not fix it since it was the international version, although they said the problem was the LCD display touch and that once we replace that part, the phone should work just like it this before. I did buy that part and the product still didn't work. it didn't even turn on this time. The worst part is that I could not even return this phone since I bough it for my sister and she lives outside the US. I don't know if the product was a fake S4 version made with cheap parts or was just bad luck. I have had the S3 and S5 and never experienced a problem like this.PLEASE DO YOURSELF A FAVOR AND DON'T BUY THIS PRODUCT, if you really want an S4, but it from a serious company where you know you can get the real product and not this probably fake stuff.

Review: I ordered this phone on March 27th from ProMobility and recieved it the very next day. Phone was working great...looked brand new from what I could tell. Unfortunately, I began noiticing a few glitches....phone wouldn't auto connect to WIFI when available, not even in my own home, the proximity sensor woudn't work and it randomly began shutting completely off on me for no reason without any warning. About last two weeks in July the phone was shutting off more often and while on a trip in South America, on August 3rd the phone never turned back on. At all! When I came back to the states a week later, I went straight to my phone service provider and the first thing the rep did is opened the back, removed the battery and showed me a sticker that shows the phone is defective due to water/liquid damage...as seen on the photo attached. Unfortunately, I am waaaaaaaaaaaay past the return period so I am at a loss. I will attempt to contact Samsung but I've been told they won't be able to anything for me either. It pisses me off that companies like ProMobility are able to sell products and totally scam people without any problems. Hope it helps.**UPDATE-I decided I wasn't accepting this as a loss...contacted Samsung directly and inquired about a replacement by warranty, but low and behold because the phone I received from ProMobility had the water/liquid damage sticker on it, Samsung explained that 12-month warranty is automatically voided...I asked if there was anything they could do because this is how I purchased the phone. Only thing they could offer was opening a repair case that I'd have to pay for. Told them thank you but no. Instead, I contacted Amazon Customer Service and within two days I am pleased to tell you that Amazon went out of their way to help me and I had a very satisfactory outcome with a full refund. Amazon was extremely prompt in responding with me and couldn't tell you how grateful I am of their helpfuness.Amazon will be invesigating ProMobility and hope that companies like ProMobility can be stopped from this selling unsatisfactory or damaged equipment.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 I337 16GB Unlocked GSM 4G LTE Smartphone, Arctic Blue w/ 13MP Camera
Review: In my opinion, this might be one of the worst purchases that I have ever made. Not only does it anger me because of the amount of money that I spent as it was a brand new phone, but it also angers & saddens me that I had to research online all the different problems that I continue to have to see how many others have/are experiencing the exact same...No one should have to ever do research like this after spending the amount of money that they do just to find out that they were basically duped, Samsung should be abundantly clear and more than willing to fix any and all manufacturing problems whether the phone is bought directly from them or through a third party carrier---After all, their name is on the phone! Yes, the phone itself works and using it outside of the problems is what I hoped for. However, when you talk on it for even just a few short moments -or- watch any type of a video feed, it quickly gets warm/hot. Next was the battery that has come with it. After pulling it off the charger at 100% (And I am electronic savvy, I have done all the little tricks to keep the battery life from draining so quickly! ), within an hour or so of NO use, the battery life is around 79% - 82%. I have had to recently order and pay out of pocket for $42 an extended life battery, which means that the $35-$40 I spent on my otter box is wasted as I won't be able to use it with my new battery. Finally comes the sim card issue. About once every 1 1/2 days, I get a message on my phone that says "Unable to detect sim card" ---Which means that I have to take the entire phone apart, pull out the battery, push the sim card in just a hair as it is only barely out, put the phone back together, and power it on so that I can use it. All these issues with a brand new phone!!!!! I really wish that I had of spent my $400 and some change on an Apple IPhone instead. I will NEVER buy another phone from Samsung, nor will I recommend them to anyone. Samsung may be a giant in the market with plenty of money to toss around, but I work two jobs just to have a nice life and that money is a lot to me!

So, we thought we were saving money with the purchases, but in reality, it was a waste of money. At least we got my money back, but not the $349 paid for the other phone. Review: Its Working great for me... "THIS PHONE IS UNLOCKED!!!!"Then i went to Walmart purchased a $45 (30 day)straight talk unlimited talk,text and data with a micro sim card.($63) After tax.when I got home,I logged on to Straight talk.com from my laptop,opened the package and had the phone and Wifi finished in about 15 min. I Then went to google play and downloaded the Apps Straight talk(My account),and Straight talk (data settings). You can then set up you address and billing info in the (MY account app). Then I went into the Data app and set up my APN (Access Point Names) settings. When you open these apps it is best to do it on your laptop so you can read the instructions and enter the changes into the phone.Its only been a couple of days,but everything has been working with no glitches!!! As for the phone.. I have owned one of these for the past 2 years and I love it. i never had any problems with it.The only reason I bought a new one was because I couldn't activate a cdma phone on a gsm network.(I had a contract with Verizon) bye bye Verizon.Very happy!!!! Review: I ordered this phone on March 27th from ProMobility and recieved it the very next day. Phone was working great...looked brand new from what I could tell. Unfortunately, I began noiticing a few glitches....phone wouldn't auto connect to WIFI when available, not even in my own home, the proximity sensor woudn't work and it randomly began shutting completely off on me for no reason without any warning. About last two weeks in July the phone was shutting off more often and while on a trip in South America, on August 3rd the phone never turned back on. At all! When I came back to the states a week later, I went straight to my phone service provider and the first thing the rep did is opened the back, removed the battery and showed me a sticker that shows the phone is defective due to water/liquid damage...as seen on the photo attached. Unfortunately, I am waaaaaaaaaaaay past the return period so I am at a loss. I will attempt to contact Samsung but I've been told they won't be able to anything for me either. It pisses me off that companies like ProMobility are able to sell products and totally scam people without any problems. Hope it helps.**UPDATE-I decided I wasn't accepting this as a loss...contacted Samsung directly and inquired about a replacement by warranty, but low and behold because the phone I received from ProMobility had the water/liquid damage sticker on it, Samsung explained that 12-month warranty is automatically voided...I asked if there was anything they could do because this is how I purchased the phone. Only thing they could offer was opening a repair case that I'd have to pay for. Told them thank you but no. Instead, I contacted Amazon Customer Service and within two days I am pleased to tell you that Amazon went out of their way to help me and I had a very satisfactory outcome with a full refund. Amazon was extremely prompt in responding with me and couldn't tell you how grateful I am of their helpfuness.Amazon will be invesigating ProMobility and hope that companies like ProMobility can be stopped from this selling unsatisfactory or damaged equipment.

Review: After careful research, I felt Samsung was the best product on the market at this time, because of its' features and design. Since I shall be traveling to Europe eminently, I wanted to be able to access the Internet for international information and communication. I am a novice with cell phone technology and am attempting to learn about my first smartphone, required SIM connections, and information available with apps via the Internet. The satelite reception for my area is poor to non-existent, so I have not rushed to purchase a smart phone. I received my package promptly. However, I thought I had a problem since both the T-Mobile sales person and I were unable to power up the phone. I'm unsure as to the sales person's problem. Yes, I was blonde in my younger days. And there is a steep learning curve for us old folks with technology.Troy with TechCell2013 very graciously offered to exchange the phone for another, pay for the extra shipping, and include extras for the inconvenience. Troy found that the phone did indeed power up and he re-shipped the phone after turtoring me with the location of the power switch. There was an unexpected delay in the re-shipment. Troy explained that he had a family emergency that detained the preparation and re-shipment. I can recommend both the Galaxy S4 unlocked phone and any purchase from TechCell2013.

Review: I don't usually write reviews but I am going to make the exception for this product. The product came on time and worked fine for 5 days then the display stopped working even though the screen did't show any crack. The phone would still turn on but the screen was completely black and I could hear some background noise. We took it to the Samsung shop and they said they could not fix it since it was the international version, although they said the problem was the LCD display touch and that once we replace that part, the phone should work just like it this before. I did buy that part and the product still didn't work. it didn't even turn on this time. The worst part is that I could not even return this phone since I bough it for my sister and she lives outside the US. I don't know if the product was a fake S4 version made with cheap parts or was just bad luck. I have had the S3 and S5 and never experienced a problem like this.PLEASE DO YOURSELF A FAVOR AND DON'T BUY THIS PRODUCT, if you really want an S4, but it from a serious company where you know you can get the real product and not this probably fake stuff.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 I337 16GB Unlocked GSM 4G LTE Smartphone, Arctic Blue w/ 13MP Camera
Review: In my opinion, this might be one of the worst purchases that I have ever made. Not only does it anger me because of the amount of money that I spent as it was a brand new phone, but it also angers & saddens me that I had to research online all the different problems that I continue to have to see how many others have/are experiencing the exact same...No one should have to ever do research like this after spending the amount of money that they do just to find out that they were basically duped, Samsung should be abundantly clear and more than willing to fix any and all manufacturing problems whether the phone is bought directly from them or through a third party carrier---After all, their name is on the phone! Yes, the phone itself works and using it outside of the problems is what I hoped for. However, when you talk on it for even just a few short moments -or- watch any type of a video feed, it quickly gets warm/hot. Next was the battery that has come with it. After pulling it off the charger at 100% (And I am electronic savvy, I have done all the little tricks to keep the battery life from draining so quickly! ), within an hour or so of NO use, the battery life is around 79% - 82%. I have had to recently order and pay out of pocket for $42 an extended life battery, which means that the $35-$40 I spent on my otter box is wasted as I won't be able to use it with my new battery. Finally comes the sim card issue. About once every 1 1/2 days, I get a message on my phone that says "Unable to detect sim card" ---Which means that I have to take the entire phone apart, pull out the battery, push the sim card in just a hair as it is only barely out, put the phone back together, and power it on so that I can use it. All these issues with a brand new phone!!!!! I really wish that I had of spent my $400 and some change on an Apple IPhone instead. I will NEVER buy another phone from Samsung, nor will I recommend them to anyone. Samsung may be a giant in the market with plenty of money to toss around, but I work two jobs just to have a nice life and that money is a lot to me!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 I545 16GB Verizon Wireless + GSM Quad-Core Android Smartphone w/ 13MP Camera - White
Review: *** UPDATED TO 1 STAR FOR LACK OF WARRANTY SUPPORT ***The display on this phone died yesterday completely out of the blue as it sat on a desk. Having purchased it new just 7 months ago, I wasn't pressed as it should be under warranty, from Samsung's Warranty Declaration:"STANDARD LIMITED WARRANTY What is covered and for how long? The warranty period commences upon the date of purchase by the first consumer purchaser and continuing for the following specified period of time after that date: Device 1 YearBattery(ies)* 1 YearOther Accessories* 1 Year"Great, I can tick all those boxes. But after going through the repair request process, I was getting charged anyway. I contacted support directly, and was told by rep Janet my phone was out of warranty "on their end". To make things worse, you can't reply, but have to start from the beginning and make a brand new support request for another random rep. And none of their survey or feedback links work.Typical of so many companies. Great product, worthless support. A phone that costs hundreds of dollars should last more than a year - and if not, it should be fixed, period.Original Review:There are many incredibly in-depth reviews for this phone as it has been around awhile. I couldn't see any reason to pay more money to get the newer version - this one has everything I wanted in a phone. The only knock I would give it is battery life. Coming from the Motorola line of 'Maxx' phones (which last for a minimum of 2 days on a charge), it was a bit of a transition. But generally, it does get through a day of my typical use. (just barely).Newer Samsung devices are Qi wireless charging compatible. This one is not - BUT you can easily add it with one of these:http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00HR10NZW/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1The S4 has the contacts (just above the battery compartment). This means installation takes no more then popping off the battery cover, slipping in the Qi adapter and aligning it with the charge contacts, and replacing the cover. 10 seconds, done. Now you have wireless charging!I can only knock this phone for two things, the battery life (though it does charge VERY quickly), and of course all the bloatware that comes installed. Thankfully you can remove or turn off all this easily - which will also help your battery life.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 I545 16GB Verizon Wireless + GSM Quad-Core Android Smartphone w/ 13MP Camera - White
Review: I needed to switch to Verizon network for reception reasons, and I needed to keep monthly payments in-line with my current service on the "un-carrier". My past experiences with S4 phones have been great and the price is now much lower than the S5, S6 or other new flagship phones. I also like the case and extended batteries available for this model. I got two phones (with nice cases and huge batteries from other sellers) for less than the price of one flagship phone.Other reviews implied that this could be a used item, although I found it listed under "new" on the web site. I have found no sign that the phones were anything less than new. One of the IMEI stickers was more difficult to read, which was a bit worrisome. Software version and everything else implied that it was a genuine SCH-I545. Kies 3 recognized and updated the phones to most recent version and they work fine.The IMEIs were accepted by the BYOD page of Verizon Wireless. They were identified as black instead of white, but I don't know if this is unusual. Unfortunately Verizon doesn't advertise that it requires double the "smartphone access fee" (jumping from $20 to $40/month!) if you bring your own device, which priced the service significantly higher than my un-carrier. This was a deal-breaker for me. Buying a new S5 phone from them was only a few dollars more, so if you are in love with Verizon customer service then that is probably the better way to go. I wanted to keep the monthly payment for my two lines at $90 or less so I was forced to decline.Rather than return the phones in defeat, I am now using Straight Talk CDMA service. The phones activated easily on the web site using kits I picked up at a local brick-and-mortar store after leaving the wireless store. So far no complaints. The service is identified as Verizon on the phone display and service was much more stable on a road trip than the un-carrier has been historically. This is according to my co-pilot who was watching youtube videos the entire 90 minutes.I can confirm the prepaid data throttling rumors. I haven't seen much more than 5 Mb/second on speed tests, which sort of negates the LTE speed advantage. It is still a decent enough speed for almost all functions. On WiFi, speed tests routinely hit 120 just like all the other S4 phones in my household.

I contacted the seller on July 13th to inquire about this issue and was informed that he would forward the issue to his "tech department" with a reply within 1 business day. It is now 10 days later and I have received no response from the seller and ended up purchasing an unlock code.The phone itself is a good product but others (who may find themselves in a similar situation as myself) need to be careful. I am led to believe that the seller has either misrepresented the product or does not stand behind his sales. I would think twice before purchasing from the seller "SNOWMAN".

Review: I knew the s4 was the phone I wanted. I needed GSM quad-band factory unlocked for use with international pre-paid SIMs (specifically European and Asian in the near future). This is the right phone. This one was for my wife and I bought another one on Amazon for myself. The one I bought for myself was technically international, it does accept SIMs from South America, all over North America, and the Caribbean. However, that other one does not accept Asian or European SIMs. Be careful what you buy on Amazon I guess is the lesson learned. Other reviews on this phone seem to state they did not get what they expected from this vendor (T-Mobile unlocked rather than truly factory unlocked, etc.). I did get exactly what I needed from this vendor, however another failed me. So, again, buyer beware. Also, don't open your phone's box in the dark because you are so excited it has arrived :-) I did that with the Americas phone and am now stuck with it.However, just to reiterate, this phone is exactly what I wanted and fit the bill perfectly. My experience with this particular phone is completely perfect.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I'm so enamored, this will likely be a multiple update review.Unboxing: Phone, documentation (which was immediately tossed aside), head phones with different sized buds, A/C charger with EU adapter, and USB data/charging cable.Setup: I pulled all the fun clings off, opened the back (easy easy), slipped in the Etisalat (AF) SIM card and SanDisk 64gb microSD. Turned it on... didn't get the normal welcome screen where I get to choose the language. Maybe I was playing with something and missed it, wound up choosing some slavic language which a friend of mine was able to read. He also identified the "life companion" title on the lock screen as French. Got through the wifi log in, changed the language and logged into Google. Normal process from there.Speakers and microphone: The sound is pretty loud when it's turned up--I was actually startled at the notification of a message. The stock keyboard and I don't get along. My hands from base of palm to middle fingertip barely reach six inches/15 cm. This is a big phone. Love it to death, but the spacing of the stock keyboard means I wind up hitting B or N instead of the spacebar, even in portrait. Personal problem, I know. I had the phone on speaker when I called Etisalat to make sure the messages I get in the future are in English, they heard me and I heard them almost as clearly as if I were using the handset directly. The rep didn't even notice the generator running in the background.Camera: The camera works ok, there was a bit of fuzz on a still I took of a friend indoors. Admittedly, the lighting wasn't all that fantastic but I think it should've come out a little better. I'm coming from a Motorola RAZR Maxx HD. Technically speaking, this camera should make the one I'm used to go cry for mummy. I'll update later when I've used it more in different situations. 12/10/2013 Update: Camera works very well. I've taken many pictures in different lighting and all of them come out very well. It helps to not be too close to what you're trying to photograph. I used Skype to see if what I see on my screen is anything like what the other end sees. Called my friend on his Motorola RAZR HD and there was little degradation/fuzziness from me to him, but it seemed like normal conversion degradation.The hardware in general makes me a bit giddy. There was some semi glitching when opening the app drawer (which disappeared when I installed Google Edition 4.3) that may have something to do with all the pre-installed apps. Videos and off-screen goodness run smoothly. I'll post updates as nifty things come up.12/10/2013 Update: I've been able to see a movie, have apps update in the background and have a phone conversation at the same time without the movie picture stuttering. I can't play a movie and have apps update on other phones. The movie loses.I can't really provide good info on the stock interface/apps/whatever. I get irritated with carrier software and extras that are pushed on me, especially when I've bought a phone outright, without a contract. If you're not in love with the Samsung extras and don't rely on warranties, the folks at xda-developers have some very informative reading.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: There a ton of reviews on this phone, so I will just explain why I chose this. Skip to the bottom if you want to know what I don't like.1st, don't believe what cnet or other sites tell you. It will always be cheaper in the end if you buy a phone unlocked. I pay 30 bucks a month for unlimited text and data with t mobile.I only get a hundred minutes, but let's be real. Free calls can be made with many Voip apps. Also, you have the freedom to use this on any GSM networks. I travel a lot, so this will save me lots of cash.2nd, I had a nexus 4. However, the stock android platform is cheap, ugly and not so user friendly.I have big hands, so my thumb always went to the home screen when trying to press the space bar. This is a boon to anyone, let alone those with sausage fingers like myself. Touch wiz has a button on the android task manager that closes all program. For stock android, you need to swipe each program out. It's a minor annoyance that adds up. It's even more frustrating that this could be a simple fix. There are all kinds of tricks that make navigating touchwiz much better than stock. If you plan on using a launcher, this probably won't matter as much to you. I used launchers in the past, and they don't really compare to built in software in my experience.3rd, better quality. This cost more than twice as much as the nexus 4. You get what you pay for! This has 16gb built in. Nexus 4 base version is 8. This has a removable batter and memory expansion slot.The battery on this lasts much longer than Nexus 4. This is impressive because of the 1080p resolution compared to the nexus 4 720p. Removing the Sim card from the Nexus 4 was a big pain. This one has an eject slot within the phone.4th, LTE. I don't know how good this works because t mobile doesn't have good lte in my area. But I like to know that it's there. Some people will say you can hack the Nexus 4 for LTE, but I don't know how true that is and not all of us have the time to spend troubleshooting this.MOST SURPRISING FEATURE: The IR port for using your phone like a remote. I'm not into TV, but I tested this out and it's kinda awesome.... It shows me a list of shows on, i tap the picture and it automatically goes to the channel. The days of flipping to find what you want are over. This works nicely with Comcast cable boxes and most televisions. I'm sure it works well with other cable boxes. I think this feature is gonna be a staple until all TV's get built in wifi.WHAT I DON'T LIKECamera: My galaxy Note 2 had zero shutter lag. this one has an annoying delay and is a step backwards despite its higher resolution.Polish software: We buy unlocked phones to avoid pre-installed software. My phone is apparently from Poland as stated in the paper. I find this kind of shady and wish I was told before buying it. HOWEVER, this software can actually be uninstalled! Unlike carrier software that may not be erased, I was actually able to remove the Polish applications without a root.S-Voice: this program is more than just a shameless knock off, it actually slows the phone down. I was upset before because my phone was so laggy. I disabled S-Voice, rebooted my phone and saw a substantial improvement.Samsung Bloat: Although Tocuhwiz is better than stock android, they are putting too much software on their phones. I wouldn't mind this if they were removable, but they are not. Many of their programs have better open alternatives. Also, if you travel internationally, Samsung is very strict on checking your sim card to assess what region you are in. This could be Samsung's eventual downfall. They need to make these apps open to all carriers and even competitors phones.Google Wallet DOES NOT WORK. Google is very strict with their wallet application, I have a Korean Galaxy Note 2 and now a Polish (International) Galaxy S4. Google wallet won't work with either even though they both contain NFC chips. Even putting American sim cards in the phones will not help. My Nexus 4 worked perfectly. So, I guess google can limits their applications by phone model. If Google Wallet is a must, skip this phone.CONCLUSION:I hate Stock android. I've had a nexus 4 and nexus 7. I love Touchwiz. I have had a galaxy s2, Note 2, and now S4. I chose this phone over the Google Play version because it's close in price, and Touchwiz is better. I work in Korea and Koreans are really good at making things that are practical to use. Stock android feels incomplete. It's mainly for developers that want to mess around with Android. If you don't want to develop, root, unlock then do yourself a favor and don't get stock android phones.I highly recommend most people to STOP doing contracts and just buy a phone outright. I have much experience in my career in many different countries around the world.These phone contracts are really really awful. This is my first time swallowing my own advice, and I don't regret it at all.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: This is not my first time buying from Amazon nor will it be the lastThe phone arrived on time and in new sealed perfect condition. All accessories are very nice except the adapter needed for US wall plugs (connections are a little loose).I was surprised at the quality of ear buds.I have three phone lines with AT&T set up for the LTE mobile 10 gig share plan.Long story short after trying a new micro SIM and working with their tech department i have been told that I can not access my data plan because either AT&T system doesn't recognize the S 4 IMEI (from the CSR/Store Manager) or that this device isn't compatible with AT&Ts LTE network (from AT&T tech department)In good locations (outside & not too far from towers) I can connect to H+ and get about 6 mg download & a little over 1 mg up.The phone is exactly what I'd like. I have to check with AT&T to find out if using a 'non' LTE device will cause any problem with my current data plan. If so, I will have to, sadly, request a return with Amazon.Also please note that there is more than one company selling this phone for close to the same price.Pros: The phone is just like everything you've seen/heard but unlocked. The battery (2600 mah) lasts over a day with heavy use IF most of the fancy Samsung settings are turned off. GPS locks are fast and very accurate (within 30 seconds and 5 meters in the house!)Cons: At first start up you have to switch from German to English. Also there are a lot of German & T-Mobile apps pre loaded and of course no AT&T LTE.If AT&T will let me keep my current plan using this phone I may not return it.I don't feel right returning something I've opened if it's not defective.If I do a return it will be my first.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I ordered an unlocked phone because I did not want to be tied to a single phone company, and moreover I do travel overseas from time to time. I have had bad experiences atempting to get other phones unlocked, both here in the US and in England. The 9505 is the S4 model sold by ATT and I therefore expected no problems in using it with ATT or T Mobile.When I unpacked the phone imagine my surprise to discover that it had come from Germany and that it was configured in German. It was easy to reconfigure for English but there are a number of applications in German that of course stayed in German and to me are not usable. The other issue is that the phone will not work in LTE mode, and to be fair to the vendor this (and only this) was declared. This is something to do with frequencies used in the US. However the phone does run in H+ mode at 5.3 Mb/s which is more than adequate. I have no regrets with my decision albeit the German was a bit of putting at first. I think the vendor, Global Mobile and or Amazon should give a paragraph explaining the German and the absence of LTE. My limited research suggests that at this time H+ is faster than LTE but may not be in the unknown future.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I've had this phone for about 4 months today, having purchase it from this supplier. The phone worked great minus some issues with the Wi-Fi which was fixed with a software update. However as of September the phone started randomly shutting down despite being fully charged. Running simple apps would also cause this as well. At first thought I was an app I installed so I factory restored the phone with no success in eliminating the problem. It so happened that I started to suspect the battery was the cause of the issue, as the battery is now swollen and there is a slight odor coming from the it. After doing some research I came to realize that the battery I have is fake (made in China rather than in Korea). Now with a swollen battery that causes the screen to lift off the phone slightly and shuts down when making a simple phone call, I am left highly dissatisfied. Who is to blame here? Do I blame FGS Trading for the fake battery I got in the box of the phone that I purchased from them or their unknown supplier. At this point I am sorely disappointed as outside of this problem the phone is a great phone.

Review: This was a fantastic phone for the three weeks that it worked. I got it the second week of February. By March 1, it was dead. Everyone at Samsung was very courteous and polite but instructed me that the phone needed to be sent back for repair, as it was not a battery or charging port issue. Two days shipping, seven-to-10 days of repair, two days shipping back. A long time to go without a phone. Another problem was that no one at Samsung USA could identify the phone by it's manufacturer's numbers. One phone support person told me the phone was built in New Zealand. Another told me it was a UK model. Both sent me to those respective websites for further support. Both were dead ends. So I gave up and sent the phone back for a refund. The refund is still in process, but the seller has been very good about communication, and I'm confident they will make good. I bought a Moto G for half the price. Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: There a ton of reviews on this phone, so I will just explain why I chose this. Skip to the bottom if you want to know what I don't like.1st, don't believe what cnet or other sites tell you. It will always be cheaper in the end if you buy a phone unlocked. I pay 30 bucks a month for unlimited text and data with t mobile.I only get a hundred minutes, but let's be real. Free calls can be made with many Voip apps. Also, you have the freedom to use this on any GSM networks. I travel a lot, so this will save me lots of cash.2nd, I had a nexus 4. However, the stock android platform is cheap, ugly and not so user friendly.I have big hands, so my thumb always went to the home screen when trying to press the space bar. This is a boon to anyone, let alone those with sausage fingers like myself. Touch wiz has a button on the android task manager that closes all program. For stock android, you need to swipe each program out. It's a minor annoyance that adds up. It's even more frustrating that this could be a simple fix. There are all kinds of tricks that make navigating touchwiz much better than stock. If you plan on using a launcher, this probably won't matter as much to you. I used launchers in the past, and they don't really compare to built in software in my experience.3rd, better quality. This cost more than twice as much as the nexus 4. You get what you pay for! This has 16gb built in. Nexus 4 base version is 8. This has a removable batter and memory expansion slot.The battery on this lasts much longer than Nexus 4. This is impressive because of the 1080p resolution compared to the nexus 4 720p. Removing the Sim card from the Nexus 4 was a big pain. This one has an eject slot within the phone.4th, LTE. I don't know how good this works because t mobile doesn't have good lte in my area. But I like to know that it's there. Some people will say you can hack the Nexus 4 for LTE, but I don't know how true that is and not all of us have the time to spend troubleshooting this.MOST SURPRISING FEATURE: The IR port for using your phone like a remote. I'm not into TV, but I tested this out and it's kinda awesome.... It shows me a list of shows on, i tap the picture and it automatically goes to the channel. The days of flipping to find what you want are over. This works nicely with Comcast cable boxes and most televisions. I'm sure it works well with other cable boxes. I think this feature is gonna be a staple until all TV's get built in wifi.WHAT I DON'T LIKECamera: My galaxy Note 2 had zero shutter lag. this one has an annoying delay and is a step backwards despite its higher resolution.Polish software: We buy unlocked phones to avoid pre-installed software. My phone is apparently from Poland as stated in the paper. I find this kind of shady and wish I was told before buying it. HOWEVER, this software can actually be uninstalled! Unlike carrier software that may not be erased, I was actually able to remove the Polish applications without a root.S-Voice: this program is more than just a shameless knock off, it actually slows the phone down. I was upset before because my phone was so laggy. I disabled S-Voice, rebooted my phone and saw a substantial improvement.Samsung Bloat: Although Tocuhwiz is better than stock android, they are putting too much software on their phones. I wouldn't mind this if they were removable, but they are not. Many of their programs have better open alternatives. Also, if you travel internationally, Samsung is very strict on checking your sim card to assess what region you are in. This could be Samsung's eventual downfall. They need to make these apps open to all carriers and even competitors phones.Google Wallet DOES NOT WORK. Google is very strict with their wallet application, I have a Korean Galaxy Note 2 and now a Polish (International) Galaxy S4. Google wallet won't work with either even though they both contain NFC chips. Even putting American sim cards in the phones will not help. My Nexus 4 worked perfectly. So, I guess google can limits their applications by phone model. If Google Wallet is a must, skip this phone.CONCLUSION:I hate Stock android. I've had a nexus 4 and nexus 7. I love Touchwiz. I have had a galaxy s2, Note 2, and now S4. I chose this phone over the Google Play version because it's close in price, and Touchwiz is better. I work in Korea and Koreans are really good at making things that are practical to use. Stock android feels incomplete. It's mainly for developers that want to mess around with Android. If you don't want to develop, root, unlock then do yourself a favor and don't get stock android phones.I highly recommend most people to STOP doing contracts and just buy a phone outright. I have much experience in my career in many different countries around the world.These phone contracts are really really awful. This is my first time swallowing my own advice, and I don't regret it at all.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: This is not my first time buying from Amazon nor will it be the lastThe phone arrived on time and in new sealed perfect condition. All accessories are very nice except the adapter needed for US wall plugs (connections are a little loose).I was surprised at the quality of ear buds.I have three phone lines with AT&T set up for the LTE mobile 10 gig share plan.Long story short after trying a new micro SIM and working with their tech department i have been told that I can not access my data plan because either AT&T system doesn't recognize the S 4 IMEI (from the CSR/Store Manager) or that this device isn't compatible with AT&Ts LTE network (from AT&T tech department)In good locations (outside & not too far from towers) I can connect to H+ and get about 6 mg download & a little over 1 mg up.The phone is exactly what I'd like. I have to check with AT&T to find out if using a 'non' LTE device will cause any problem with my current data plan. If so, I will have to, sadly, request a return with Amazon.Also please note that there is more than one company selling this phone for close to the same price.Pros: The phone is just like everything you've seen/heard but unlocked. The battery (2600 mah) lasts over a day with heavy use IF most of the fancy Samsung settings are turned off. GPS locks are fast and very accurate (within 30 seconds and 5 meters in the house!)Cons: At first start up you have to switch from German to English. Also there are a lot of German & T-Mobile apps pre loaded and of course no AT&T LTE.If AT&T will let me keep my current plan using this phone I may not return it.I don't feel right returning something I've opened if it's not defective.If I do a return it will be my first.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I knew the s4 was the phone I wanted. I needed GSM quad-band factory unlocked for use with international pre-paid SIMs (specifically European and Asian in the near future). This is the right phone. This one was for my wife and I bought another one on Amazon for myself. The one I bought for myself was technically international, it does accept SIMs from South America, all over North America, and the Caribbean. However, that other one does not accept Asian or European SIMs. Be careful what you buy on Amazon I guess is the lesson learned. Other reviews on this phone seem to state they did not get what they expected from this vendor (T-Mobile unlocked rather than truly factory unlocked, etc.). I did get exactly what I needed from this vendor, however another failed me. So, again, buyer beware. Also, don't open your phone's box in the dark because you are so excited it has arrived :-) I did that with the Americas phone and am now stuck with it.However, just to reiterate, this phone is exactly what I wanted and fit the bill perfectly. My experience with this particular phone is completely perfect.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I'm so enamored, this will likely be a multiple update review.Unboxing: Phone, documentation (which was immediately tossed aside), head phones with different sized buds, A/C charger with EU adapter, and USB data/charging cable.Setup: I pulled all the fun clings off, opened the back (easy easy), slipped in the Etisalat (AF) SIM card and SanDisk 64gb microSD. Turned it on... didn't get the normal welcome screen where I get to choose the language. Maybe I was playing with something and missed it, wound up choosing some slavic language which a friend of mine was able to read. He also identified the "life companion" title on the lock screen as French. Got through the wifi log in, changed the language and logged into Google. Normal process from there.Speakers and microphone: The sound is pretty loud when it's turned up--I was actually startled at the notification of a message. The stock keyboard and I don't get along. My hands from base of palm to middle fingertip barely reach six inches/15 cm. This is a big phone. Love it to death, but the spacing of the stock keyboard means I wind up hitting B or N instead of the spacebar, even in portrait. Personal problem, I know. I had the phone on speaker when I called Etisalat to make sure the messages I get in the future are in English, they heard me and I heard them almost as clearly as if I were using the handset directly. The rep didn't even notice the generator running in the background.Camera: The camera works ok, there was a bit of fuzz on a still I took of a friend indoors. Admittedly, the lighting wasn't all that fantastic but I think it should've come out a little better. I'm coming from a Motorola RAZR Maxx HD. Technically speaking, this camera should make the one I'm used to go cry for mummy. I'll update later when I've used it more in different situations. 12/10/2013 Update: Camera works very well. I've taken many pictures in different lighting and all of them come out very well. It helps to not be too close to what you're trying to photograph. I used Skype to see if what I see on my screen is anything like what the other end sees. Called my friend on his Motorola RAZR HD and there was little degradation/fuzziness from me to him, but it seemed like normal conversion degradation.The hardware in general makes me a bit giddy. There was some semi glitching when opening the app drawer (which disappeared when I installed Google Edition 4.3) that may have something to do with all the pre-installed apps. Videos and off-screen goodness run smoothly. I'll post updates as nifty things come up.12/10/2013 Update: I've been able to see a movie, have apps update in the background and have a phone conversation at the same time without the movie picture stuttering. I can't play a movie and have apps update on other phones. The movie loses.I can't really provide good info on the stock interface/apps/whatever. I get irritated with carrier software and extras that are pushed on me, especially when I've bought a phone outright, without a contract. If you're not in love with the Samsung extras and don't rely on warranties, the folks at xda-developers have some very informative reading.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: I ordered an unlocked phone because I did not want to be tied to a single phone company, and moreover I do travel overseas from time to time. I have had bad experiences atempting to get other phones unlocked, both here in the US and in England. The 9505 is the S4 model sold by ATT and I therefore expected no problems in using it with ATT or T Mobile.When I unpacked the phone imagine my surprise to discover that it had come from Germany and that it was configured in German. It was easy to reconfigure for English but there are a number of applications in German that of course stayed in German and to me are not usable. The other issue is that the phone will not work in LTE mode, and to be fair to the vendor this (and only this) was declared. This is something to do with frequencies used in the US. However the phone does run in H+ mode at 5.3 Mb/s which is more than adequate. I have no regrets with my decision albeit the German was a bit of putting at first. I think the vendor, Global Mobile and or Amazon should give a paragraph explaining the German and the absence of LTE. My limited research suggests that at this time H+ is faster than LTE but may not be in the unknown future.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: For all you people asking, "Where will this phone work?" Stop asking and do some simple research. go to gsmarena and search for the i9505.Then go to wiki and find out what frequency your carrier runs on. It will be under the NETWORK heading. For instance ATT in the US uses 850 and 1900 for GSM and 700,1700 and 2100 for LTE. Do a tiny bit of research before posting dumb questions.This phone was a tough choice over the HTC one.Reasons over HTC were:Removable battery ( a must for modders at times)Super AMOLED screen is amazing with a great resolutionCamera is very good even in lower light settingsSound quality is not terrible at all.This phone also weighs less then it appears which is a nice feature.International- any network, anytime, to include LTE.For the average user they will surely love this phone in how smooth it is stock.I also recommend this over the i9500 for the faster cpu with longer lasting battery with the snapdragon processorThe only bad thing was a lack of a search button which now appears on nearly every page.If you are into modding. With Foxhounds rom and Adam's kernel found on XDA i only lose about 9% battery on extremely limited use with wifi and several widgets(including temperature, humidity, cpu temp speeds music player, notepad and power toggles.)On very heavy use to include music, a few games and texts while on WiFi the battery only dropped about 30% after 3.5 hours. I can easily go 1.5 - 2 days on medium usage. Xda also has a thread for speaker enhancement to get the volume louder both on headphones and through the speaker. This with a ICS theme make this phone perfect.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 i9505 16GB LTE Unlocked International Version White
Review: This product had European firmware that had someone singing on the start up.Then phone booted, and it would not read ANY similar card.had to buy a new sim card / sd card reader from eBay $11.Take the phone apart, use a heat gun to separate the adhesive, unplug the required connector, then put in the new part and re stick the new sim card reader down. Then put all 9 screws back in and cross my fingers it all worked.I bought this phone for my housemate to replace his iPhone 5 because my Galaxy S4 is awesome.I have read that the no sim card installed error is common on the S4. Luckily I was technologically minded and experienced enough to fix it my self. Took a month from ordering phone to getting it working after waiting for replacement parts. Plus now the sim card does not click in to place, but at least it works.I flashed new firmware and rooted the phone to see if it was a region lock problem not reading the sim, in so voiding the warranty and having fix it myself.i can't give a good rating for these reasons.Best phone on the market, till Galaxy S5, but too many people online with the same sim problem, which just tells me that Samsung do not have the best hardware testing........

Review: Lost a star due to battery life, even though the low battery life was to be expected out of the box it simply sucks too much battery. Am using easy battery saver now and it helps a lot. The phone also packs quite a bit of heat from the screen and from the back it self, not sure if that can be fixed thru software but if you are planning to make a lot of long calls, you might want to consider a blue tooth device in addition to this phone.Camera, pics looked okay on the phone out of the box but can be grainy when you start to view these on a larger display. There might be some settings that are changed to get the hi res pictures that are advertised on gsm arena. But after some tinkering the camera can look great.Features and quality.Excellent screen does not get better than super amoled in my opinion, and resolution is top notch.Phone calls and texting feels as they should and sounds as they should.Beautiful finish and the white looks just perfect.Advanced features like the air and smart gestures work but I have not gotten the smart pause to work just yet. People are usually amazed by these features especially when little is known about the phone itself.In the end it is a great phone,with some unique features and high quality that needs some adjustments to get 5 stars but with the adjustments you can have lots of quality fun and experiences.Hope this was helpful.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini Duos GT-i9192 Factory Unlocked International GSM Dual Sim Cell Phone - Retail Packaging - Black
Review: I bought this for the dual SIM facility, since I make regular visits to the UK and need to be available to friends and family there without them having to dial a US number. That aspect of it works reasonably well. However, there are three serious problems with it. 1 - It keeps forgetting all the email settings, and will suddenly behave as if the email accounts were never configured, and I have to do it all again from scratch (a chore, as I have four accounts I need the phone to be able to use). 2 - It will suddenly and spontaneously reboot itself, for no apparent reason. 3 - Its process can't cope with running a GPS app for in-car navigation. When using the phone for navigation, it will suddenly freeze (requiring the battery to pull and be reinserted to get it to work again) or reboot itself again. Same thing happens with Waze and Google navigation apps, so it isn't a software issue. Furthermore, after a few minutes of running a navigation app, the phone gets so hot you could fry an egg on the back of it.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-I9192 GSM Factory Unlocked Dual Sim - 8GB (Black Leather)
Review: Amazing Phone. I loved the reviews and here's one more to help you.PRO: You can do it all on a dual sim.. I save TONS getting rid of my land lines and having a dual sim. US market sucks cause they do not offer this, but its okay, you can use the unlocked Euro phone in US. Incredible flexibility and you can say bye bye to unwanted landline bills.CON: Major one is the battery life.. I use aps and its always being use so battery drains fast. I suggest getting an extender I am trying out Slim External Rechargeable Backup Battery Charger Charging Case Cover for Samsung Galaxy S4 mini with Pop-Out Kickstand (3000mAh) but have not yet tried it to review how much life extension it gives. I feel it could make the phone more normal on battery power. Another con is the small screen. Going from a S3 large screen to s4 Mini was like going backwards on size, but its okay because the phone fits again in my pocket easier like a phone should!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: I was very bothered when Samsung changed the options button when the S5 came out. So I prefer to go for this one, I has been a month since I bought it. Is great for me, I don't need the last most expensive cellphones but is modern enough to look cool and can do basically everything a student needs. So far the battery last almost all day long with a regular use although it takes a very long time to recharge. I haven't take it to its limits in the sense that I use only a few basic apps (Whatsapp, WatchON, Uber, etc). Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: UPDATE: The Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini (i9195) does not get LTE service on T-Mobile.It's hugely disappointing. My recommendation would be to buy the Motorola X phone if LTE is an absolute must.=================This Galaxy S4 Mini cell phone is intriguing. While on one hand it represents the Galaxy S4 line. It is a different beast in several respects: 1. It is smaller and much easier to one hand thumb swipe. 2. It has a beautiful clear screen albeit smaller than the S4 and wonderful responsiveness the capacitive touch sensitivity.That being said, I lament the loss of a physical keyboard (I came from a Motorola XPRT, Android 4.0) and a Palm Pre before that and a Blackberry before that. On any of those devices I could start typing and the contextual aspects would present an on-screen list of relevant items. For example, type "Brent" and each of those devices would start doing a universal search and present a link for BRENT phone call link, BRENT last SMS message, BRENT latest email, BRENT calendar appointment, BRENT contact record, BRENT Google search results,The S4M requires 3 taps in different areas of the screen before the keyboard is even presented. It's far more work to get to where I want to go than in my other phones.All that said, the S4M is leaps and bounds faster, more elegant, and more beautiful in almost all areas compared to my other phones EXCEPT the Palm Pre.The other aspects of this phone that are interesting is that it comes in several versions: 1. HSPA+ w LTE (what I have), 2. Two (2) GSM slots + LTE. (what I wanted if I was on AT&T) 3. CDMA + LTE (What I wanted if knew it worked with Sprint). I'm enjoying the T-Mobile services (i.e., free hotspot, wi-fi calling).I'd say the main drawback to the phone is all of the Samsung software that has been added on top of Android 4.2.2. So many things that have great opening screens but the integration and elegance of use with other Samsung apps or other Android apps has not been achieved.In short, I'd buy it again. I'm very happy with it after 1 month. While I believe it is hard for any review to achieve a perfect 5. This one certainly falls short in areas mentioned above and comes out strong on other items.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: There are many good reviews and a few bad so I decided to weigh the odds and purchase. The phone was in good cosmetic condition upon arrival.Upon startup, the default language was French. This was an alarm as I had noted from another reviewer that the local Samsung repair center (Caribbean) would not service these as they are from another zone.Worse: the receiver speaker was not working! After some digging around, I realised that there was a bad connection at the speaker which would work if the speaker was pressed firmly against the case. This appeared to be an uncommon problem globally and I contemplated contacting the supplier for action but realised that cost of return within warranty period may exceed the cost of the unit since I reside in the Caribbean.I decided to forego the warranty and dismantled the phone and confirmed my speaker was faulty. I was able to pad the space between the speaker and the case and reassemble. It works fine now!This is the risk that Caribbean shoppers take when purchasing directly from the USA but it usually is still cost beneficial. Perhaps I just got a bad apple from the orchard.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: This phone is truly a great phone. Yet, it lacks some of the features that it's older brother (The s4) has. Such as the notification light, and air gesture. The phone is a beautiful phone, and has a cheap sort of feel- that's only because it's backing is plastic which is better for if you drop it so that's a plus. The phone has a nano sim slot- there are different versions of the s4 mini such as the I9190 and I9192 so each sim for each phone has different requirements. The phone is pretty fast, although a few months in it freezes sometimes. Perhaps, that's due to the fact that I am constantly on it. The camera is okay, it is nothing special but the fact it has two cameras front and back (has a flash light) is great. Phone calls are clear, and the service I get is pretty good considering the phone is not from the US and hasn't even made it here yet. The fact the phone is unlocked enables you to use any service you please- not being bound to a service is nice. The price as of now is high, but not as high as an iphone, considering it is unlocked raises the price. Over all I love my phone! If you have any questions please ask!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: Okay. I was definitely worried about buying this phone at first because it was an international, factory unlocked phone. But, I really wanted the phone so I decided to go with it. When I got the phone, it looked awesome! The only sucky part was that the instruction manuals were all in French... But, Í can just watch reviews on YouTube about it. No problem. However, when I went to put my SIM card in, it would not fit. It was WAY too big for it because the phone takes a microSIM card. So crap. But I was so determined to use the phone I was not going to let that get me down. I contacted my local provider- I read reviews from other people on here that said to do so- but they weren't much help. This one lady was like "Oh, you just break open your SIM card and the micro one I inside of it". I was like umm okay that's not gonna happen. So, I googled it and then found this DIY on cnet that instructs you how to make a microSIM card out of your regular SIM card by cutting it. I felt really adventurous and decided to give it a try because if worse came to worse I would just have to pay $25 for a microSIM. So,I watched the video, followed the instructions, and I did it! It was not hard to do at all. Then, I took my freshly cut miroSIM card and put it in the phone. It fit perfectly. I turned the phone on and first it couldn't find my provider but then I selected a different option in the service section in setting and up popped my provider. This phone is amazing and is so fast and fun to use. Definitely worth the money. Love it!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: WOW. This phone actually fits in your hand! Although it has a 4.3" screen, it is narrower than the original Galaxy S1 (Captivate, Vibrant, etc.) as well as the Galaxy S3 mini. What else? Great specs! This is a really fast and powerful phone, WITH an MicroSD card slot. While not as fast as the full-sized S4, it is faster than the S3, and many other phones on the market. That, along with TouchWiz UI, plus the option to install CyanogenMod (Stock Android ROM), and you have a really nice phone. The only cons are the screen resolution and the bundled software. The screen is not HD, but really, it's fine. 16:9 videos look great and fit edge-to-edge. As far as software, the phone comes preloaded with a lot of garbage that cannot be removed without rooting, but it still runs fast, and the apps can be hidden from the app drawer. The phone also has NFC, eye tracking, and plenty of other gimmicks... and the price is right, so what's there not to like?

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: I needed a lighter phone than my Google Nexus and was very close to getting an iPhone 5 due to the size and weight, but thankfully a colleague told me about the S4 mini before switching over! This phone is absolutely awesome. I am one of those people who HATE big screen phones, so this is a Godsend for me. It's fast. Perfect size. Light. Built extremely well for a "mini" model. Many many features. It's the perfect iPhone killer and has a much lower radiation output too.Its SAR rating (radiation level) is hardly 0.33 watts per kilogram of body mass (on a scale of 0 to 1.60!). Phones with such a low SAR rating are very rare, and much lower than the iPhone 5 which is a whopping 1.6 watts per kilogram!The best part, it's UNLOCKED so you can use tethering all you want with no extra charges. Just be warned, LTE may not work on many carriers so check that ahead of time. Mine runs on AT&T HSPA+ and I'm happy with the speed.Don't listen to the bad reviews here. People complaining about the Micro SIM are just delusional, any major carrier will provide a new SIM for free. Just make sure you get a SAMSUNG SEALED PHONE or send it back! The first one I received was not sealed and wasn't even LTE. Amazon exchanged it for the right one. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: If you're lucky and you get this phone, it's great. But BEWARE of fraudulent sellers (e.g. S***TY_Services_WORST_Practices)Let's face it. Someone buying am "UNLOCKED International Version No Warranty " phone, is doing it for only one reason: To use it outside the US.So a crook seller sees an oportunity to commit fraud: Let's sell them some $50 used cel phone with a sticker that says i9195 for $300 and when they noticed they where ripped off, they'll be out of the country and won't be able to complain.That was almost my story. Luckily I was able to notice ON TIME, that the model number behind the battery was for an used S7562 (a $50 cel) and not a new i9195 (a $300 one). The worst was that this is a fraud and not an error, since the sticker outside said i9195. They know what they where doing, and they do it on purpose, to criminally cheat us. I tried to contact the seller, but they didn't want to solve the problem, they even insulted me. Luckly, I contacted Amazon and they refunded me the money and I bought it again in another seller, and got the i9195 I wanted (which is great). But I was almost ripped of by this "Quality_Services_Best_Practices" crooks. Do you want to risk buying from crooks like these?Read the other reviews for this phone. And you'll se some happy people and some angry ones. This is not due to the phone itself. Some sellers are S***TY_Services_WORST_Practices and they commit fraud to people, hoping they can't complaing from abroad.The i9195 is a GREAT phone, just google i9195 review and you'll see. But you are reading this review, because you pay for the phone YOU GET, and at first I didn' get what I ordered.Be extremely aware from what seller you buy this "international" phone, because some are just waiting to rip you off

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: This review has been applied to the wrong phone by Amazon. It is applicable to the black S4 Mini "international" version. After having the phone now for ten months and being warned during question and answer emails prior to purchase that this was NOT a 4G LTE phone, I can attest that it is NOT a 4G LTE phone. Specifically, not on the Straighttalk BYOP AT&T provider network to which my phone is connected. In these ten months, I have never experienced a connection indicated as greater than H+ which I believe is akin to 3G. I am not a power user, and use at my cabin in northern Wisconsin where the phone is the connection to the outside world, the signal available is so weak that true AT&T phones strain to obtain a signal. Having a 4G LTE phone where a 4G signal is not available, is not a big advantage. It would have been nice to have a phone as advertised when used in a 4G LTE service area.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: It does what I've been looking in a smart phone. It'll run graphically intensive games like Real Racing 3 with no problems at all. Doing that will suck down the battery.... about 10% for 20 ish minutes of playing... If you're smart with your phone and keep the brightness down and do other things to conserve battery, it'll last for quite some time. I use mine moderately for listening to music and playing clash of clans and things of that nature and only have to charge it every 2-4 days or so... It depends on how much you use it.One thing that weirds me out is that while this happens rarely, when i go to bed, it'll be at like 90%-100% and I'll wake up and it will be at 50%. It doesnt happen very often. It happened to my android tablet sometimes (It wasnt a samsung, it was a cheapo one) so i'm guessing it was an android os type of thing where it didnt go into sleep mode properly or something.... but it doesnt really take away from the phone :)It fits in my hand perfectly and I don't have to do any hand gymnastics to reach a portion of the screen but still has a plenty big screen to show detail. The model I received was actually GT-i9195i (note the i at the end(EDIT: silly me, the title says i9195i >_> lol) which has a quad core processor and an amoled screen.It only had 8 gb of internal memory, but with that sd card slot, I now have an additional 32 gb (I could get more) with plenty of space to spare :DThe camera takes decent pictures and video too :)But nevertheless, great phone :) 5stars

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: I've had the phone for nearly half a year now. It is a great phone. I cannot say how it handles games, cause I do not play any games. When it comes to browsing with more than 20 tabs open, I haven't experienced any lags. It plays videos really well without any problems. I can stream any videos without any problems, as well. Plays music without any problems and I don't have any problems with other computations.The one star deduction is for Samsung, because they do not support phones that have been taken out of the country of where it comes from. So you are left all alone unless you can contact a Samsung representative from the original country that the phone is from. I would, also, like to say that as someone who roots their phone, you will not be able to receive software updates for your phone and void the warranty, but that is a given. I'm not saying there aren't any methods.WARNING:I bought my phone from a reseller called FGS Trading and I had the worst experience. First off, the shipping box that the phone came in was 10x times bigger than the actual phone and it was completely smashed. It didn't stop there, the phone I received had been already used and FGS Trading gave a power adapter (because it's an international phone and plugs overseas are different than The Untied States), that had hair on it. It was inside the crevices of the power adapter. I was so disgusted. I immediately contacted them and they did everything to try and avoid their responsibilities as a reseller to fix the problem. First their excuse was that they are a small company and no would dare to put any hair, etc. Then, I asked what about the box and the phone being used? They just said that I can just get a refund back, but then I'd have to send the phone back and pay for the shipping. I then, sent them pictures of how the phone came in... You know how they replied? "It seems you must be hurt by this." I then sent an angry message back for their lack of responsibility and how they are dismissing the points of how I received the phone. They were completely making it seem like I was the problem for everything. I immediately contacted Amazon... Then, FGS contacted me back saying, "Oh, I see you've contacted Amazon and are going to give us a bad name and reputation. You didn't give us enough time. You immediately contacted them. " I'm like wanting to strangle someone now and reply back to them, "Listen F* I gave you a week and a half and Amazon has a 30 day protection return policy, I am not waiting around. " I got a hold of an Amazon representative and they said that sense I didn't buy it through Amazon, I would have to return the phone, pay the shipping, and they will help to make sure I get a full refund. I got upset and said I'm not paying for s***. I just want a new phone. So, in my midst of effort, I finally got sent a new phone after contacting a Samsung representative of where my phone came from. Didn't pay for anything.That is it. After all that experience, I was happy at the end.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: The phone arrived yesterday. Google tells me the language of the quick start guide is Czech, the box and the software installed on it is from the Orange carrier. I order from Cell-Source, even though it was little pricier than some of the other orders listed because it was Amazon Prime and the percentage of positive reviews posted was a few percent higher than other sellers. I was still a little nervous about it because of seeing other reviews with unnamed sellers that were not unlocked and because the price for the phone on amazon.fr after applying the conversion made me think there can't be much profit in the business of selling unlocked phones.The phone arrived Friday in an unopened box. I charged it and it turned on just fine. I didn't get a chance to verify that it was unlocked that night since the sim card from my (dying) dumb phone wasn't the right size/type for this phone. But the next day I took it to Tmobile and there were zero problems switching my number and a new sim to the phone.I might update on the phone as I get more use out of it. For the moment I am happy to report that the product that I received was as advertised. Unlocked, international, functioning.
