Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: Until now, i have just 2 days using it, and have no complaints. The only one was that i couldn't get it on time and i had to pay another $63 to send it to my country. It took a week to get from CA to NY, which I considered was a lot of time, I had a family member in NY that had to bring it to my country, and because of that delay, my cousin left and i had to pay extra fee to send it by other carrier to my country. (I WROTE THIS REVIEW WHEN I HAD JUST 2 DAYS USING THE PHONE)AFTER 4 DAYS OF USE:When i decided to buy this phone, i checked all the reviews and the questions made to the seller and i found out, it works in my country because it match with my frequency and GHz. I bought it to use it out of USA, but I had a family that was able to bring it to my country. Due to a problem with the delivery, my cousin could not bring the phone when she returned and because of that, my aunt had to send me the phone by UPS to my country. I was avoiding paying extra fees, but at the end, I had to pay $73.30 dollars of extra fees to have it in my country. Because of that, i received the phone 28 days later than when i made the purchase.Anyway, I get the phone and i started use it and i was working very well the first 4 days, after that, the phone start to fail, the screen remained paralyzed without making any movement, and none of the functions worked up after a while of being immobile, one of the days, the battery overheat and i had to pull out the battery fast and leave the phone off for around 12 hours.I contacted the seller telling the situation, thinking that maybe was the battery, the told me how warranty works with Samsung and the gave the steps to follow. The fact that i was out of USA, in Costa Rica, I had to contact Samsung Latin America and the will give me the places in my Country authorized to cover warranties of the Samsung items. The gave me the numbers and i went to the place hoping the will help out with the problem of the cell phone and of course using the warranty, because the phone was New, only 4 days of use. When I went to the place, they told me they were not able to help me due to 2 things: 1- The IMEI of the phone belong to Europe specific FRANCE and in my country the only cover warranties of IMEI's that belong to Latin America, that way the weren't able to cover the warranty, neither can fix the phone if I was willing to pay for it, because they can not even fix item that doesn't belong to Latin America, that way I had no choices to can fix the phone in my country.When I told the seller what happened, they never tried to help me, they just sent me an e-mail saying they will not cover the warranty and that was it. I had to speak with Amazon customer service to put a complain, and because of that, they tried to be in touch with me again (the seller), but the fact that the phone had more than 30 days since the day of the purchase, they were not able to received the phone back due to the returns policies, even when i explained that I received the phone 28 days later of the purchase and I had just 4 days using it when it started to fail.TO ALL BUYERS: PLEASE BE AWARE, when buy a phone like this one, ask the seller first the IMEI and double check if that belong to the country you live before to make the purchase, because, i know that most of the buyers think that the sellers have to cover warranties or be responsible for defecting items, but in this case Samsung works different and warranty is based on the IMEI of the phone, and they give support and warranties only if the IMEI belongs with your country. In my case, I received a damage item and now i can't have warranty neither support..... and the seller was not what i expected.. they were not kind, neither polite, very disappointed with the seller.To all Buyers, before to make an expensive purchase, please make sure you know everything about all this issues that happened to me, that way you will not lose the money. PLEASE BE CAREFUL!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost

I specially love it because it really beats even the new iphone 5c,5s with only 107 compared to 132,113 that are recently released. It obviously has a bigger screen, 4.3 inch compared to 4 inch in all three versions of iphone 5 as it is still easy to carry around and fits in your pocket easily. I never had an issue with the battery life. The pictures are vivid and has a much more powerful front camera (1.9 M) in comparison to iphone 5 (1.2 M). The back camera is just the same. If you are not just a blind fond of iphone, you wouldn't throw away all that money in two years!! The 5C version which is supposed to be cheap, is $550 and you easily run out of the 16 GB memory without being able to extend it. With this phone, you can easily buy an SD card of up to 64GB and you are good to go.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: Okay. I was definitely worried about buying this phone at first because it was an international, factory unlocked phone. But, I really wanted the phone so I decided to go with it. When I got the phone, it looked awesome! The only sucky part was that the instruction manuals were all in French... But, Í can just watch reviews on YouTube about it. No problem. However, when I went to put my SIM card in, it would not fit. It was WAY too big for it because the phone takes a microSIM card. So crap. But I was so determined to use the phone I was not going to let that get me down. I contacted my local provider- I read reviews from other people on here that said to do so- but they weren't much help. This one lady was like "Oh, you just break open your SIM card and the micro one I inside of it". I was like umm okay that's not gonna happen. So, I googled it and then found this DIY on cnet that instructs you how to make a microSIM card out of your regular SIM card by cutting it. I felt really adventurous and decided to give it a try because if worse came to worse I would just have to pay $25 for a microSIM. So,I watched the video, followed the instructions, and I did it! It was not hard to do at all. Then, I took my freshly cut miroSIM card and put it in the phone. It fit perfectly. I turned the phone on and first it couldn't find my provider but then I selected a different option in the service section in setting and up popped my provider. This phone is amazing and is so fast and fun to use. Definitely worth the money. Love it!

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: WOW. This phone actually fits in your hand! Although it has a 4.3" screen, it is narrower than the original Galaxy S1 (Captivate, Vibrant, etc.) as well as the Galaxy S3 mini. What else? Great specs! This is a really fast and powerful phone, WITH an MicroSD card slot. While not as fast as the full-sized S4, it is faster than the S3, and many other phones on the market. That, along with TouchWiz UI, plus the option to install CyanogenMod (Stock Android ROM), and you have a really nice phone. The only cons are the screen resolution and the bundled software. The screen is not HD, but really, it's fine. 16:9 videos look great and fit edge-to-edge. As far as software, the phone comes preloaded with a lot of garbage that cannot be removed without rooting, but it still runs fast, and the apps can be hidden from the app drawer. The phone also has NFC, eye tracking, and plenty of other gimmicks... and the price is right, so what's there not to like?

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: I needed a lighter phone than my Google Nexus and was very close to getting an iPhone 5 due to the size and weight, but thankfully a colleague told me about the S4 mini before switching over! This phone is absolutely awesome. I am one of those people who HATE big screen phones, so this is a Godsend for me. It's fast. Perfect size. Light. Built extremely well for a "mini" model. Many many features. It's the perfect iPhone killer and has a much lower radiation output too.Its SAR rating (radiation level) is hardly 0.33 watts per kilogram of body mass (on a scale of 0 to 1.60!). Phones with such a low SAR rating are very rare, and much lower than the iPhone 5 which is a whopping 1.6 watts per kilogram!The best part, it's UNLOCKED so you can use tethering all you want with no extra charges. Just be warned, LTE may not work on many carriers so check that ahead of time. Mine runs on AT&T HSPA+ and I'm happy with the speed.Don't listen to the bad reviews here. People complaining about the Micro SIM are just delusional, any major carrier will provide a new SIM for free. Just make sure you get a SAMSUNG SEALED PHONE or send it back! The first one I received was not sealed and wasn't even LTE. Amazon exchanged it for the right one. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!!

Review: If you're lucky and you get this phone, it's great. But BEWARE of fraudulent sellers (e.g. S***TY_Services_WORST_Practices)Let's face it. Someone buying am "UNLOCKED International Version No Warranty " phone, is doing it for only one reason: To use it outside the US.So a crook seller sees an oportunity to commit fraud: Let's sell them some $50 used cel phone with a sticker that says i9195 for $300 and when they noticed they where ripped off, they'll be out of the country and won't be able to complain.That was almost my story. Luckily I was able to notice ON TIME, that the model number behind the battery was for an used S7562 (a $50 cel) and not a new i9195 (a $300 one). The worst was that this is a fraud and not an error, since the sticker outside said i9195. They know what they where doing, and they do it on purpose, to criminally cheat us. I tried to contact the seller, but they didn't want to solve the problem, they even insulted me. Luckly, I contacted Amazon and they refunded me the money and I bought it again in another seller, and got the i9195 I wanted (which is great). But I was almost ripped of by this "Quality_Services_Best_Practices" crooks. Do you want to risk buying from crooks like these?Read the other reviews for this phone. And you'll se some happy people and some angry ones. This is not due to the phone itself. Some sellers are S***TY_Services_WORST_Practices and they commit fraud to people, hoping they can't complaing from abroad.The i9195 is a GREAT phone, just google i9195 review and you'll see. But you are reading this review, because you pay for the phone YOU GET, and at first I didn' get what I ordered.Be extremely aware from what seller you buy this "international" phone, because some are just waiting to rip you off

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: UPDATE: The Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini (i9195) does not get LTE service on T-Mobile.It's hugely disappointing. My recommendation would be to buy the Motorola X phone if LTE is an absolute must.=================This Galaxy S4 Mini cell phone is intriguing. While on one hand it represents the Galaxy S4 line. It is a different beast in several respects: 1. It is smaller and much easier to one hand thumb swipe. 2. It has a beautiful clear screen albeit smaller than the S4 and wonderful responsiveness the capacitive touch sensitivity.That being said, I lament the loss of a physical keyboard (I came from a Motorola XPRT, Android 4.0) and a Palm Pre before that and a Blackberry before that. On any of those devices I could start typing and the contextual aspects would present an on-screen list of relevant items. For example, type "Brent" and each of those devices would start doing a universal search and present a link for BRENT phone call link, BRENT last SMS message, BRENT latest email, BRENT calendar appointment, BRENT contact record, BRENT Google search results,The S4M requires 3 taps in different areas of the screen before the keyboard is even presented. It's far more work to get to where I want to go than in my other phones.All that said, the S4M is leaps and bounds faster, more elegant, and more beautiful in almost all areas compared to my other phones EXCEPT the Palm Pre.The other aspects of this phone that are interesting is that it comes in several versions: 1. HSPA+ w LTE (what I have), 2. Two (2) GSM slots + LTE. (what I wanted if I was on AT&T) 3. CDMA + LTE (What I wanted if knew it worked with Sprint). I'm enjoying the T-Mobile services (i.e., free hotspot, wi-fi calling).I'd say the main drawback to the phone is all of the Samsung software that has been added on top of Android 4.2.2. So many things that have great opening screens but the integration and elegance of use with other Samsung apps or other Android apps has not been achieved.In short, I'd buy it again. I'm very happy with it after 1 month. While I believe it is hard for any review to achieve a perfect 5. This one certainly falls short in areas mentioned above and comes out strong on other items.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: There are many good reviews and a few bad so I decided to weigh the odds and purchase. The phone was in good cosmetic condition upon arrival.Upon startup, the default language was French. This was an alarm as I had noted from another reviewer that the local Samsung repair center (Caribbean) would not service these as they are from another zone.Worse: the receiver speaker was not working! After some digging around, I realised that there was a bad connection at the speaker which would work if the speaker was pressed firmly against the case. This appeared to be an uncommon problem globally and I contemplated contacting the supplier for action but realised that cost of return within warranty period may exceed the cost of the unit since I reside in the Caribbean.I decided to forego the warranty and dismantled the phone and confirmed my speaker was faulty. I was able to pad the space between the speaker and the case and reassemble. It works fine now!This is the risk that Caribbean shoppers take when purchasing directly from the USA but it usually is still cost beneficial. Perhaps I just got a bad apple from the orchard.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: This phone is truly a great phone. Yet, it lacks some of the features that it's older brother (The s4) has. Such as the notification light, and air gesture. The phone is a beautiful phone, and has a cheap sort of feel- that's only because it's backing is plastic which is better for if you drop it so that's a plus. The phone has a nano sim slot- there are different versions of the s4 mini such as the I9190 and I9192 so each sim for each phone has different requirements. The phone is pretty fast, although a few months in it freezes sometimes. Perhaps, that's due to the fact that I am constantly on it. The camera is okay, it is nothing special but the fact it has two cameras front and back (has a flash light) is great. Phone calls are clear, and the service I get is pretty good considering the phone is not from the US and hasn't even made it here yet. The fact the phone is unlocked enables you to use any service you please- not being bound to a service is nice. The price as of now is high, but not as high as an iphone, considering it is unlocked raises the price. Over all I love my phone! If you have any questions please ask!

Review: This is a great phone. Very responsive with a vivid display. The phone size hits the sweet spot. Large enough to work with apps, videos, etc. while small enough to be handled easily with one hand. While the larger phones are better suited to watch videos, I'm more interested in using it with useful apps and, of course, as an actual phone..... :) Key features of this phone that sold me is the ability to add up to 64gb of storage with its micro sd slot and the removable battery.It's great to easily be able to replace a failing battery, or simply be able to carry a spare battery if you find yourself unable to recharge your phone when your traveling. I purchased 2 unlocked phones and one of the phones was not unlocked when I received it. I've seen others complain about this and having to pay to get them unlocked. No need to pay to fix this. I called up the Seller (Open Wireless Group) and they gave me the unlock pin number over the phone. If your Seller does not do this for you, simply return the phone to Amazon since it was not what you ordered.

Way better than what the Iphone 4 was getting. Best part of this is no more Itunes!

Review: This review is critical review and NOT intended to degrade the sellers' reputation in any way.The packaging was good... my package included:- 2 back covers 1. branded with the carrier's logo 2. plain white- ear phones- charger - with NO ADAPTER- battery- phone (UNLOCKED) and is good working condition so far.- manualThe customer service was adequate. I sent two queries while my order was processing and in both instances someone responded.The response was not diplomatic & friendly as if the employee were trained in customer service BUT it was a response nonetheless.However I am absolutely PEEVED about the fact that my charger did not come with an adapter. How is this possible? This phone is supposed to be an international version i.e. it should carry an adapter, after all it is intended for international use. Needless say, I will now be forced to purchase an adapter to ensure my phone can be charged.To follow on from this... other colleagues in my office also purchased the same exact phone but from DIFFERENT SELLERS and they did not encounter this issue. When each phone was unboxed, every box had an adapter enclosed.SHOPEBEST, your service was not bad, the phone works well, however I would like to suggest that the international version of this phone be sourced from one of the other regions listed on the box. That means the package will come with adapter included, hopefully SHOPEBEST would not need to incur additional cost.I hope this review is helpful.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini GT-i9195i Unlocked Cellphone, Retail Packaging, White Frost
Review: The biggest disappointment to me is the screen. I understand that Samsung needed to reduce the size in order to shrink the phone's footprint, but it dialed the resolution back to 2011. The rest of the hardware looks good and functions well. Voice calls are generally good, signal performance was adequate, and battery life delivers a day's worth of use.The software is nearly as advanced as the full-sized phone, but nixes some of the features for reasons unknown. For example, Smart Stay and Smart Scroll are gone, but you can still use Face Unlock. The TouchWiz user interface is as flexible (and complex) as ever. The camera offers plenty of neat features, but loses the GS4's most exciting capture modes. Thank goodness the Mini takes good pictures. Media options are plentiful, even though the GS4 Mini doesn't have the best screen on which to watch movies.To wrap-up its for what you get: a smaller, more user-friendly handset that isn't quite the flagship it pretends to be.

Review: It does what I've been looking in a smart phone. It'll run graphically intensive games like Real Racing 3 with no problems at all. Doing that will suck down the battery.... about 10% for 20 ish minutes of playing... If you're smart with your phone and keep the brightness down and do other things to conserve battery, it'll last for quite some time. I use mine moderately for listening to music and playing clash of clans and things of that nature and only have to charge it every 2-4 days or so... It depends on how much you use it.One thing that weirds me out is that while this happens rarely, when i go to bed, it'll be at like 90%-100% and I'll wake up and it will be at 50%. It doesnt happen very often. It happened to my android tablet sometimes (It wasnt a samsung, it was a cheapo one) so i'm guessing it was an android os type of thing where it didnt go into sleep mode properly or something.... but it doesnt really take away from the phone :)It fits in my hand perfectly and I don't have to do any hand gymnastics to reach a portion of the screen but still has a plenty big screen to show detail. The model I received was actually GT-i9195i (note the i at the end(EDIT: silly me, the title says i9195i >_> lol) which has a quad core processor and an amoled screen.It only had 8 gb of internal memory, but with that sd card slot, I now have an additional 32 gb (I could get more) with plenty of space to spare :DThe camera takes decent pictures and video too :)But nevertheless, great phone :) 5stars

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini L520 16GB Sprint CDMA 4G LTE Android Smartphone - Black

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini L520 16GB Sprint CDMA 4G LTE Android Smartphone - Black
Review: This is not a Sprint phone. It is a Boost phone that operates on the CDMA network which Sprint has.It CAN be used with the Sprint prepaid system, or the Boost prepaid system. But it can only be used on these prepaid systems.Also, the phone did not come with a UICC card. It took some time and research, but I was able to find the right one on Ebay and buy it for 9.00.The item description was ...Sprint Boost Virgin Micro SIM Card for Galaxy Tab 3, S4 Mini HTC Desire 207R.The important part is "207R." This is not a SIM card as it is thought of for the GSM phones. It is only used to enable the phone on the LTE network somehow. It does not contain personal information or contacts.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 Mini L520 16GB Sprint CDMA 4G LTE Android Smartphone - Black

But it can only be used on these prepaid systems.Also, the phone did not come with a UICC card. It took some time and research, but I was able to find the right one on Ebay and buy it for 9.00.The item description was ...Sprint Boost Virgin Micro SIM Card for Galaxy Tab 3, S4 Mini HTC Desire 207R.The important part is "207R." This is not a SIM card as it is thought of for the GSM phones. It is only used to enable the phone on the LTE network somehow. It does not contain personal information or contacts.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SCH-I545 16GB Arctic Blue - Verizon Wireless
Review: *** UPDATED TO 1 STAR FOR LACK OF WARRANTY SUPPORT ***The display on this phone died yesterday completely out of the blue as it sat on a desk. Having purchased it new just 7 months ago, I wasn't pressed as it should be under warranty, from Samsung's Warranty Declaration:"STANDARD LIMITED WARRANTY What is covered and for how long? The warranty period commences upon the date of purchase by the first consumer purchaser and continuing for the following specified period of time after that date: Device 1 YearBattery(ies)* 1 YearOther Accessories* 1 Year"Great, I can tick all those boxes. But after going through the repair request process, I was getting charged anyway. I contacted support directly, and was told by rep Janet my phone was out of warranty "on their end". To make things worse, you can't reply, but have to start from the beginning and make a brand new support request for another random rep. And none of their survey or feedback links work.Typical of so many companies. Great product, worthless support. A phone that costs hundreds of dollars should last more than a year - and if not, it should be fixed, period.Original Review:There are many incredibly in-depth reviews for this phone as it has been around awhile. I couldn't see any reason to pay more money to get the newer version - this one has everything I wanted in a phone. The only knock I would give it is battery life. Coming from the Motorola line of 'Maxx' phones (which last for a minimum of 2 days on a charge), it was a bit of a transition. But generally, it does get through a day of my typical use. (just barely).Newer Samsung devices are Qi wireless charging compatible. This one is not - BUT you can easily add it with one of these:http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00HR10NZW/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1The S4 has the contacts (just above the battery compartment). This means installation takes no more then popping off the battery cover, slipping in the Qi adapter and aligning it with the charge contacts, and replacing the cover. 10 seconds, done. Now you have wireless charging!I can only knock this phone for two things, the battery life (though it does charge VERY quickly), and of course all the bloatware that comes installed. Thankfully you can remove or turn off all this easily - which will also help your battery life.

Review: I needed to switch to Verizon network for reception reasons, and I needed to keep monthly payments in-line with my current service on the "un-carrier". My past experiences with S4 phones have been great and the price is now much lower than the S5, S6 or other new flagship phones. I also like the case and extended batteries available for this model. I got two phones (with nice cases and huge batteries from other sellers) for less than the price of one flagship phone.Other reviews implied that this could be a used item, although I found it listed under "new" on the web site. I have found no sign that the phones were anything less than new. One of the IMEI stickers was more difficult to read, which was a bit worrisome. Software version and everything else implied that it was a genuine SCH-I545. Kies 3 recognized and updated the phones to most recent version and they work fine.The IMEIs were accepted by the BYOD page of Verizon Wireless. They were identified as black instead of white, but I don't know if this is unusual. Unfortunately Verizon doesn't advertise that it requires double the "smartphone access fee" (jumping from $20 to $40/month!) if you bring your own device, which priced the service significantly higher than my un-carrier. This was a deal-breaker for me. Buying a new S5 phone from them was only a few dollars more, so if you are in love with Verizon customer service then that is probably the better way to go. I wanted to keep the monthly payment for my two lines at $90 or less so I was forced to decline.Rather than return the phones in defeat, I am now using Straight Talk CDMA service. The phones activated easily on the web site using kits I picked up at a local brick-and-mortar store after leaving the wireless store. So far no complaints. The service is identified as Verizon on the phone display and service was much more stable on a road trip than the un-carrier has been historically. This is according to my co-pilot who was watching youtube videos the entire 90 minutes.I can confirm the prepaid data throttling rumors. I haven't seen much more than 5 Mb/second on speed tests, which sort of negates the LTE speed advantage. It is still a decent enough speed for almost all functions. On WiFi, speed tests routinely hit 120 just like all the other S4 phones in my household.

A regular 500 mhAmp charger will charge this but much more slowly and the phone will tell you. It is 1/4 as fast at charging as a 2 amp charger. The phone has learned my writing style and can suggest words as I type texts and emails. The on screen keyboard is larger than some and has a permanent numbers row on top like a computer keyboard. After my last Samsung phone I bought an extra battery and battery charger, but haven't opened them. It is usually very responsive to touch and is so easy to operate for this reason. Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SCH-I545 16GB Arctic Blue - Verizon Wireless
Review: *** UPDATED TO 1 STAR FOR LACK OF WARRANTY SUPPORT ***The display on this phone died yesterday completely out of the blue as it sat on a desk. Having purchased it new just 7 months ago, I wasn't pressed as it should be under warranty, from Samsung's Warranty Declaration:"STANDARD LIMITED WARRANTY What is covered and for how long? The warranty period commences upon the date of purchase by the first consumer purchaser and continuing for the following specified period of time after that date: Device 1 YearBattery(ies)* 1 YearOther Accessories* 1 Year"Great, I can tick all those boxes. But after going through the repair request process, I was getting charged anyway. I contacted support directly, and was told by rep Janet my phone was out of warranty "on their end". To make things worse, you can't reply, but have to start from the beginning and make a brand new support request for another random rep. And none of their survey or feedback links work.Typical of so many companies. Great product, worthless support. A phone that costs hundreds of dollars should last more than a year - and if not, it should be fixed, period.Original Review:There are many incredibly in-depth reviews for this phone as it has been around awhile. I couldn't see any reason to pay more money to get the newer version - this one has everything I wanted in a phone. The only knock I would give it is battery life. Coming from the Motorola line of 'Maxx' phones (which last for a minimum of 2 days on a charge), it was a bit of a transition. But generally, it does get through a day of my typical use. (just barely).Newer Samsung devices are Qi wireless charging compatible. This one is not - BUT you can easily add it with one of these:http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00HR10NZW/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1The S4 has the contacts (just above the battery compartment). This means installation takes no more then popping off the battery cover, slipping in the Qi adapter and aligning it with the charge contacts, and replacing the cover. 10 seconds, done. Now you have wireless charging!I can only knock this phone for two things, the battery life (though it does charge VERY quickly), and of course all the bloatware that comes installed. Thankfully you can remove or turn off all this easily - which will also help your battery life.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: This model of the Samsung Galaxy S4 is technically unlocked, but it is an AT&T phone which means a few things:-If you do not have an AT&T sim card, you will not be able to update the software via Software Update in settings. You will be stuck on Android version 4.4.2 KitKat-Rooting the phone is a bit more difficult, considering AT&T does not want you to-You cannot install Cyanogenmod-AT&T bloatware and logo on back of phoneOther than that, the phone is amazing. Runs very smoothly, loads better than my previous device, an iPod 5, and the AMOLED display is fantastic. Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: This review will be less about the product itself (because we all know this is an amazing, powerful, fast, feature-packed flagship smartphone) and more about SIM Compatibility/Network. Because that's why we're looking at unlocked phones, right?I digress. In June, I bought the Straight Talk Samsung Galaxy SIII from WalMart for $459.99. I thought it an amazing purchase that I would not regret... I was so wrong. Little did I know it was a CDMA phone (locked to Straight Talk) and it ran on the SPRINT network. This meant little to me then, but it means the world to me now, because I now know that Sprint is the most awful, bogged-down, spotty, insert-derogatory-phrase-here network on this entire planet. I live in Hawaii, and Sprint's network in Hawaii is an absolute joke. I had no service at my own house OR my workplace that was right on the convergence line of two Sprint towers (Sprint can't switch towers very easily so just went into roaming). The closest Sprint tower to my house had a coverage radius of roughly 1 mile, and still does. And worst of all, it's a 3G-only network. Not a regular 3G network, either. I got EDGE speeds at best. I've never pulled more than 1Mbps on the up or down. Straight Talk's S3 was also very watered down, with a locked bootloader and very limited software accessibility. It was a sad life for me.I had had it. I was done with this pitiful excuse for a carrier. All my friends had either AT&T or Verizon, who both had full LTE buildouts here. They always pulled webpages and videos like lightning. Their coverage was broad and consistent. I figured it was time to pinch a few pennies. I had heard that Straight Talk had released an LTE-capable AT&T SIM Card, and I needed a phone for it to power. I had my heart pretty much set on the S4, but didn't know which one to get.This was the one.The product arrived brand-new and completely unopened. Shiny new S4. I put it in the wall, popped the SIM in, edited the APN settings ([...]) and BAM. The 4G LTE icon popped up in the status bar, as well as two consistent bars of service. My experience thus far has been wonderful - I've been pulling a pretty steady 15up/19down everywhere I've been thus far. I now have coverage in my home. I have yet to see the NO SERVICE icon. This may not be a problem for many of you as you may be already coming from AT&T's network - but it's a whole different world for me.TL;DR? This phone works perfectly with Straight Talk's AT&T Micro SIM card. There is no hassle and no fuss - I set it up entirely online in 10 minutes. If these qualifications meet your criteria, buy this sucker - you won't be disappointed.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: Let me tell you the truth about this phone, and also about what is being offered vs what you get. I work in the Telecom market so I know the details about this phone.The phone itself is great. The problem is the type of S4 you are gettingFirst thing, this phone is NOT unlocked, once you insert a SIM card different from ATT, it will tell you that the phone is locked and you need to insert the PIN code. So if you want to use a different SIM from other carrier-country, you need to find-buy the code from the internet, then the phone is fully unlocked and any SIM will work.Second thing I encounter when trying to use a T-Mobile SIM Card. The AWS bands of T-Mobile for 3G/4G HSPA+ service are closed so you need to look on the Internet to find a way to open it if you want to have coverage on those technologies.Phone connects to T-Mobile LTE only after I dialed a number !!!Bottom line you are not getting a fully operating unlockable phone that can be used anywhere.You have to play with the configuration and search-change its configuration to make it work as a fully unlocked phone to be used with any SIM Card worldwide.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: A tad bigger than the Samsung Skyrocket, this phone is fast. It has neither crashed, nor shown any lag. The screen and back of the phone can get a bit hot to touch. I find this happens with most Android phones I have owned (i.e. Captivate, Skyrocket, S4), especially if you have the back light raised to maximum output for longer than several minutes.A word of caution: if you are a developer or like to root your devices, you should be aware that you need the MDB/MDL firmware versions of the phone in order to install a recovery. If you have MF3, firmware, you will not be able to install recovery and it will be trick to root the device. I believe "safe strap" is the answer but I do not know the details. Look into this issue before purchasing. Further, if you do purchase a device and it is rootable, your carrier will likely try to push an over the air update to your firmware changing it to MF3 or beyond. If you want to root and install a recovery, you will have to stop this process. I advise you also look into this.Update Nov 2013: I believe there is a way to root and install a recovery on S4s with MF3 or later firmware. One method is called "Safe Strap."

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I'd give it ZERO stars if I could. Spent close to $300 for a phone they claimed had only been in use 14 days, had been tested and was working. Well, they didn't test it well because ironically after the 30 day return option had expired, the phone started to glitch out. Thought it was a bad battery, ordered one online, when it came, I made the swap. That only put a band aid on the issues which seemly grew worse as time went on. The screen started to act up among other issues, then it died. Did what anyone would do, put it on the charger, overnight. When I awoke the next day, still wouldn't come back on. When I found a place that could take a closer look it, I was told that the battery was fully charged, the screen was operational and almost all other components were functioning as normal. Notice I mentioned ALMOST all other components? The motherboard, the vital part of this phone's operating system, was FRIED. Not from sitting in the sun, not from taking a swim, just fried. So, when this seller states something was fully tested and that it's only been used 14 days, think twice about buying it. I get that it didn't come with a warranty, however, when selling electronics, it is necessary to fully disclose the condition of these units so that the buyer is fully informed. Had the statement read "the phone is a year old, has been used as intended but might have operational issues" I would have passed on this one for a another less questionable unit. So BUYER BE AWARE!!!! Not everything coming from this seller will be as it appears or is as advertised and if you are a single mom like me that needed communication options but was not really wanting to part with $300, just keep your money and save it a few more months to buy something new and with a warranty. DON'T BUY FROM THIS SELLER. Simply send them a check with a note 'Merry Chirstmas', free money for you. I'm sure they will take it, no questions asked.

Review: I moved from S2 to S4 and I'm really happy with it as I was with the S2. The screen is really big and clear without the phone being as bulky as the Note. I wear this on my belt. The new features work fine. The 13MP camera looks amazing as you take pictures. The problem is that you have to take pictures very still. You almost need to use a tripod. If you move just a little, the picture ends up blurry. Now the video it takes looks just amazing.The battery doesn't last more than half a day, but I do use it a lot for internet, videos and games so I'm used to charging it all night and at work. The processors are really fast and you now have a lot of memory. Enough to have multiple big programs open without issue. I used to have some issues installing many apps in my S2 because of small internal memory (not everything could be moved to the SD). I haven't had to worry about that with the S4 yet. I also tested the screen mirroring feature with a nice Samsung tv and there was some lag but it was OK. When watching a video, you can just send the video instead of the screen and then there is no lag. I also considered the S5 but this one was $400 and the S5 was more than $600.Overall really happy with it. If I learn how to configure the camera so the shutter is faster or it compensates for slight hand movements I will never let go of this phone.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I don't know why I waited so long to buy this phone. I kept my last phone for over 6 years (Motorola Q9C) and it had always been nearly worthless for doing anything online, although I could get my e-mail through it and do some limited surfing if I had enough time and patience. I had an unbelievably cheap service plan that I was reluctant to give up (couldn't upgrade the phone and keep the plan). But I finally threw in the towel, and now I'm asking myself why I waited so long. This phone does EVERYTHING.The one thing I was reluctant about was typing on a touchscreen, which I have difficulty with. But then I realized I don't even need to type, I can dictate e-mails and texts! And it works great! I'm using it with Straight Talk on AT&T's network and it's been flawless, with 4g LTE speeds (need the correct APN settings to get LTE). The battery life has been good (although I am not a heavy user), and on standby it's phenomenal, it seems to barely use any power at all. The sound quality is great, the screen is great, and as each day passes and I learn more and more about how to use this phone, the happier I get with it.Prior to buying this I had read quite a bit about the fake phones out there and was determined to not be fooled into getting one of those. I did a lot of research about what to look for, and everything I read about this phone indicated it was the real deal. But one never knows until they get it in their hands. After receiving it I carefully examined everything, then downloaded and ran the "Genuine Galaxy - Phone Info" app which confirmed that the phone is authentic. What a relief. I bought this from seller "Mango Wireless," fulfilled by Amazon. It was brand new, in Samsung packaging. The only thing that seemed to be missing from the box was a warranty card (there were manufacturer's pamphlets explaining the warranty - but no card). It was listed in the description as being "Imported," which for cameras means there is no USA warranty. Not sure if that means the same thing for phones, but it probably does. I could have gotten the add-on 2-year protection plan for an additional $69, but decided to go without that. I figured I could send it back to Amazon within 30 days if need be, but if it ran fine during that time it would be unlikely to go bad over the succeeding 11 months anyway (Samsung's usual warranty is 1 year). I did get an Otterbox Defender case for it to provide some extra accident protection. Some may find that case to be too bulky, but I love it. I've had the phone for 3 weeks now and it is working perfectly.One of my passions is long-distance self-supported bicycle touring, and in the past I have taken a netbook, phone, mp3 player, camera and GPS with me, which adds quite a bit of weight to my setup. All of that will now be replaced with just this phone - awesome! This will lower my riding weight by 5 or 6 pounds and reduce the volume of supplies I need to carry. I am going to test it out on a tour starting in one week and will update this review with how it all worked out.I couldn't be happier with this phone.//UPDATE 12/7/2014// - The phone is still flawless. I've had it for 5 months now and still love it. No issues at all. On the bike tour it worked great. I kept an online blog, updating it only with this phone. I also used it to watch movies at night in my tent - I had about 25 movies copied to the memory card. The movies played flawlessly and the sound was good enough to not need earphones, but I often used earphones anyway. A 90-minute movie used about 20% of the battery charge which I thought was pretty good. No regrets.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I had to pay 15.00 to an unlocking service to use it on T-Mobile. 4 stars for the phone and NO stars for the seller Wireless Experts, Inc. A quick note, too: S4 I337 is a phone made for use with AT&T, not T-Mobile, even though both provides are GSM. "Unlocked" means you SHOULD be able to use it for both providers, which is clearly not the case here.Now, on to what matters: the phone. This is my first Samsung It's a lot snappier than my all 3 of my generic BLU Studio phones. It's plenty fast for what I use it primarily for, namely, browse the Web and stream music and videos. I went with an older model as I didn't think it was worth shelling out 2-3 times more for an S5 or S6 for what I gather to be a marginal (imperceptible?) improvement in speeds.No problems with this phone for texting, emailing, and phone calls. It's 4G LTE but I only get 2-3 Mbps when I did a benchmark on the speedtest (.net) site, but then again, that what I got on my BLU Studio 4G LTE phones, so it must be a T-Mobile network issue, not the phone. Still, 2-3 Mbps is plenty for streaming full HD videos on the phone's 1920 x 1080 resolution (441 dpi if I'm not mistaken).Additionally, text is sharp and images are sharp and vibrant. The view angles are absolutely stunning. There is no drop-off in visibility at viewing angles close to 180 degrees on all four sides. There is a tiny ghosting problem, however, perceptible only with a dark background and light foreground -- not a deal breaker by any means.Plus, unlike the S6, the phone has a removable/replaceable battery. I usually bring a portable battery charger with me but I like the option of switching out the spent battery for a fresh one if I need to.For basic usage like phone calls, texting, social media, browsing the Web and for more CPU-intensive usage like Full HD video streaming and music streaming, this phone performs like a champ. If these are what you plan on using your phone primarily for, I'd definitely recommend this over the S5 or S6 and definitely over the Note series. It may be an older model but it NOT outdated for sure. I expect it to provide faithful service for many years to come.

Review: The phone gets 2 stars because it worked fine for 2 months. Almost to the day. Then, without warning it stopped reading the sim card (ATT customer) rendering the phone useless. I contacted the seller and I returned the phone for repair or replacement. I received my phone back yesterday after the seller replaced the motherboard. This review is not for the seller, only the phone itself. I went to the local ATT store (hated only 2nd to Walmart) and had phone/sim card reactivated. All seemed well. Everything seemed to be working. Then about an hour or so later my son called. I couldn't hear him and as it turns out he could not hear me. Thankfully, the texting worked and after many calls, texts, restarting and playing around with this phone, it was obvious the hearing/ear/microphone was not working. Defective.Back to hated ATT store to reactivate my trusty old phone (thank goodness I have one) and called the seller. To sum up, I've been sent a return label and this piece of crap phone is going back again.Do yourselves a favor and don't buy this phone. I'd like to make it clear this review is for the phone only and not the seller. I will leave feedback for the seller once this issue is resolved. Which, by the way things are going, may not be for awhile.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: The product itself is amazing. This phone runs fast with a clear bright screen amazing camera, sound and design. The size and weight its preferred too and I can't help but fall in love with this model. I bet you are wondering why I rated one star however. The reason being is because What I received was only but a shell of what it should have been. I did not get a sim card OR a warranty booklet that is to be included in the box. I also had noticed that the charger port of the phone was unreliably loose. Due to my past experience with Samsung devices I figured that was common and decided to roll with it. Turns out the phone also takes forever to charge. I really hope that' that's just my copy of the phone and not an issue with the model itself because a full nights sleep only got it from 0%-80% and using the phone constantly with the phone light with most of the power saving apps and features only gives me about 4-6 hours. That's frustrating cause I had just dropped the I phone 5 (cause of my displeasure with the Apple software and restrictions) and the IPhone could take less than an hour to charge fully and could last me a whole day.after about 2 nights of having this product the third night I get home from work with this phone under 3% of battery. I shut it off and plug in the charger for about 18 minutes as I watch a show on my computer so it could charge at its fastest. After one episode I checked the phone to see its charge and it wouldn't light up with the fancy battery icon and the LED was not on. I had realized that the phone was unresponsive, didn't light up, wouldn't show any sign of charge and worst of all wouldn't TURN ON. I tried many methods of trouble shooting to figure out why my new cellular device wasn't working and none had worked. Some told me to take out the battery and hold the power button, some told me to let It sit on the charger a while, I was also recommended to press and hold all the buttons for a certain amount of time and none of it worked. I called AT&T to complain and they had convinced me to do a return on this product so when I logged on to amazon I saw my phones battery icon appear and start charging again. I figured that I was supposed to leave it on the charger for a lot longer than what the previous website suggested if that's the reason why it came back to life. I was literally 2 seconds away from shipping this back and getting my money back, I give it a few more days to stop being unreliable before I demand a FULL return for this defective device because I am not spending the initial 200 dollars and what I pay a month for THIS.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I've tried my phone for almost 6 months and it's been nothing but trouble from day 1. I had issues when I first ordered one it didn't connect to any of my laptops and there was a malfunction. Then I was sent another one and now this one has problems. The screen freezes more frequently now, the texting features don't work. I have not dropped this phone, but it sure feels like that this had problems since the day I got it. I should have returned it completely instead of getting a replacement. I bought this as my S3 charging port went bad after 3 years and I didn't have any other problems other than that. I should've just replaced the charging port and it could've resolved my issues. I'm highly disapointed that after spending almost $200 and my phone's already malfunctioning.

Review: Update! One advantage of a refurbished AT&T phone on an AT&T network is that software updates are timely. I no sooner had the phone set up than I had a notice from AT&T that an upgrade was available to take the phone from Kit Kat .4.2 to Lollipop 5.0.1. I am pleased.I'm not going to say much about the phone itself. It's fabulous, and ther are plenty of very good reviews on Amazon. My issue is with the item description. Nowhere in the item description was it noted that this was an (unlocked) AT&T phone. I love the S4, but I really didn't want all that AT&T bloatware. My previous S4 was an AT&T, and I had disabled and hid most of those apps. When my phone crashed, I went looking for a NON AT&T replacement. Imagine my disappointment when my phone finally arrived and I saw that logo on the back, knowing I was stuck with that startup screen and tone and the ton of bloatware on the phone. If the S4 were not such a wonderful phone, I'd return this and keep looking.

You will be stuck on Android version 4.4.2 KitKat-Rooting the phone is a bit more difficult, considering AT&T does not want you to-You cannot install Cyanogenmod-AT&T bloatware and logo on back of phoneOther than that, the phone is amazing. Runs very smoothly, loads better than my previous device, an iPod 5, and the AMOLED display is fantastic. It's almost as if I was colourblind using my iPod until I saw true colours for the first time on this phone. The screen size is also excellent.Overall, if you can afford to go a hundred dollars higher, I recommend getting the international version of this phone.

I just didn't see much in the way of improvements in opting for the S5 or other current flagship over the S4. I even considered the One Plus One 64GB and had an invite. But the supplies are limited, they are in a fight with software provider (CyanoGen) and accessories are limited. Overall I am very happy with my choice. It does everything I need it too and without lag. Most of the newer "Entry" to "Mid" phones are alright and come with newer processors and specs. But most only come with 1GB of RAM. Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: Great phone, but not "new" as listed.The phone listing said:Samsung Galaxy S4 I337 16GB 4G LTE GSM Smartphone - AT&T (Black)Sold by: Greenleaf Mobile (seller profile)Condition: NewThe phone arrived in early July, 2014, looking and performing great. It came with no warranty, and for about 3 months it worked fine. Then all of a sudden I started getting random SIM card error messages "SIM card not detected. Please restart device" . I would take the SIM card out, gently wipe it with a soft cloth and re- insert it. This would happen from 1 to 5 times daily. As I run my business on my phone, this is a very big problem for me.There is no AT&T Store anywhere close to where I live, but I called AT&T multiple times to see if they could give me some information. Went through all the various trouble shootings, but the problem has just persisted. I have never dropped the phone or had water on it. An AT&T tech tracked the IMEI number and informed me that the phone had been activated the first time in September of 2013 and had been used on another network, 10 months before I receive it, sold to me as "NEW":I then contacted the Seller three times, but they were not interested in communicating with me. I presented all the information I had and included a copy of the written message I received from AT&T, stating the original activation date. I asked how they wanted to remedy the error, and was told I was mistaken, they had sold me a new phone.A second email to Greenleaf Mobile got me the reply that "do not mistake imei, it can be issued to different phones".A third email to Greenleaf Mobile was replied with " we sold you 100% new phone".I then contacted Amazon. As this sale was Fulfilled by Amazon, they are backing me up and are refunding me my money so that I can purchase a phone that actually works. THANK YOU AMAZON!Please beware when purchasing cell phones without a warranty. Please beware of purchasing cell phones not Fulfilled by Amazon.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I had to pay 15.00 to an unlocking service to use it on T-Mobile. 4 stars for the phone and NO stars for the seller Wireless Experts, Inc. A quick note, too: S4 I337 is a phone made for use with AT&T, not T-Mobile, even though both provides are GSM. "Unlocked" means you SHOULD be able to use it for both providers, which is clearly not the case here.Now, on to what matters: the phone. This is my first Samsung It's a lot snappier than my all 3 of my generic BLU Studio phones. It's plenty fast for what I use it primarily for, namely, browse the Web and stream music and videos. I went with an older model as I didn't think it was worth shelling out 2-3 times more for an S5 or S6 for what I gather to be a marginal (imperceptible?) improvement in speeds.No problems with this phone for texting, emailing, and phone calls. It's 4G LTE but I only get 2-3 Mbps when I did a benchmark on the speedtest (.net) site, but then again, that what I got on my BLU Studio 4G LTE phones, so it must be a T-Mobile network issue, not the phone. Still, 2-3 Mbps is plenty for streaming full HD videos on the phone's 1920 x 1080 resolution (441 dpi if I'm not mistaken).Additionally, text is sharp and images are sharp and vibrant. The view angles are absolutely stunning. There is no drop-off in visibility at viewing angles close to 180 degrees on all four sides. There is a tiny ghosting problem, however, perceptible only with a dark background and light foreground -- not a deal breaker by any means.Plus, unlike the S6, the phone has a removable/replaceable battery. I usually bring a portable battery charger with me but I like the option of switching out the spent battery for a fresh one if I need to.For basic usage like phone calls, texting, social media, browsing the Web and for more CPU-intensive usage like Full HD video streaming and music streaming, this phone performs like a champ. If these are what you plan on using your phone primarily for, I'd definitely recommend this over the S5 or S6 and definitely over the Note series. It may be an older model but it NOT outdated for sure. I expect it to provide faithful service for many years to come.

Review: The phone gets 2 stars because it worked fine for 2 months. Almost to the day. Then, without warning it stopped reading the sim card (ATT customer) rendering the phone useless. Everything seemed to be working. Then about an hour or so later my son called. I couldn't hear him and as it turns out he could not hear me. Thankfully, the texting worked and after many calls, texts, restarting and playing around with this phone, it was obvious the hearing/ear/microphone was not working. Defective.Back to hated ATT store to reactivate my trusty old phone (thank goodness I have one) and called the seller. Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: The product itself is amazing. This phone runs fast with a clear bright screen amazing camera, sound and design. The size and weight its preferred too and I can't help but fall in love with this model. I bet you are wondering why I rated one star however. The reason being is because What I received was only but a shell of what it should have been. I did not get a sim card OR a warranty booklet that is to be included in the box. I also had noticed that the charger port of the phone was unreliably loose. Due to my past experience with Samsung devices I figured that was common and decided to roll with it. Turns out the phone also takes forever to charge. I really hope that' that's just my copy of the phone and not an issue with the model itself because a full nights sleep only got it from 0%-80% and using the phone constantly with the phone light with most of the power saving apps and features only gives me about 4-6 hours. That's frustrating cause I had just dropped the I phone 5 (cause of my displeasure with the Apple software and restrictions) and the IPhone could take less than an hour to charge fully and could last me a whole day.after about 2 nights of having this product the third night I get home from work with this phone under 3% of battery. I shut it off and plug in the charger for about 18 minutes as I watch a show on my computer so it could charge at its fastest. After one episode I checked the phone to see its charge and it wouldn't light up with the fancy battery icon and the LED was not on. I had realized that the phone was unresponsive, didn't light up, wouldn't show any sign of charge and worst of all wouldn't TURN ON. I tried many methods of trouble shooting to figure out why my new cellular device wasn't working and none had worked. Some told me to take out the battery and hold the power button, some told me to let It sit on the charger a while, I was also recommended to press and hold all the buttons for a certain amount of time and none of it worked. I called AT&T to complain and they had convinced me to do a return on this product so when I logged on to amazon I saw my phones battery icon appear and start charging again. I figured that I was supposed to leave it on the charger for a lot longer than what the previous website suggested if that's the reason why it came back to life. I was literally 2 seconds away from shipping this back and getting my money back, I give it a few more days to stop being unreliable before I demand a FULL return for this defective device because I am not spending the initial 200 dollars and what I pay a month for THIS.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I'd give it ZERO stars if I could. Spent close to $300 for a phone they claimed had only been in use 14 days, had been tested and was working. Well, they didn't test it well because ironically after the 30 day return option had expired, the phone started to glitch out. Thought it was a bad battery, ordered one online, when it came, I made the swap. That only put a band aid on the issues which seemly grew worse as time went on. The screen started to act up among other issues, then it died. Did what anyone would do, put it on the charger, overnight. When I awoke the next day, still wouldn't come back on. When I found a place that could take a closer look it, I was told that the battery was fully charged, the screen was operational and almost all other components were functioning as normal. Notice I mentioned ALMOST all other components? The motherboard, the vital part of this phone's operating system, was FRIED. Not from sitting in the sun, not from taking a swim, just fried. So, when this seller states something was fully tested and that it's only been used 14 days, think twice about buying it. I get that it didn't come with a warranty, however, when selling electronics, it is necessary to fully disclose the condition of these units so that the buyer is fully informed. Had the statement read "the phone is a year old, has been used as intended but might have operational issues" I would have passed on this one for a another less questionable unit. So BUYER BE AWARE!!!! Not everything coming from this seller will be as it appears or is as advertised and if you are a single mom like me that needed communication options but was not really wanting to part with $300, just keep your money and save it a few more months to buy something new and with a warranty. DON'T BUY FROM THIS SELLER. Simply send them a check with a note 'Merry Chirstmas', free money for you. I'm sure they will take it, no questions asked.

However, I have straight talk with at&t towers so when I called straight talk to get phone connected, it worked. But,... This phone IS an AT&T phone, not a pure, straight from Samsung, unlocked phone. So it is not unlocked it the sense you can use it with any carrier because this phone was made by AT&T for AT&T. Im pretty sure my phone is new, but when I got it, the back case was half open and there was debris under the plastic protector on the back of the phone. The screen protector was on the screen, it peeled off clean and everything in the box appeared new and unopened. I even got Samsung earbuds. If the phone works, I'll keep it, but I thought this was a Samsung unlocked phone, not an AT&T unlocked phone.Minus one star because of this and because of the back case open and debris. I'll update for the good or bad in future if necessary. I actually started the return process and printed out return labels because the box said it was locked. Seeing now as I got it working, I'll keep it for bit and try it out before making a decision on to keep or return.I do like that this phone has removable battery as most if all new phones these days don't... That is just stupid.***update 9-15-15***I'm returning. Just bought this phone Don straight talk as it is a straight Samsung phone without the stupid at@t boatware.

Review: I moved from S2 to S4 and I'm really happy with it as I was with the S2. The screen is really big and clear without the phone being as bulky as the Note. I wear this on my belt. The new features work fine. The 13MP camera looks amazing as you take pictures. The problem is that you have to take pictures very still. You almost need to use a tripod. If you move just a little, the picture ends up blurry. Now the video it takes looks just amazing.The battery doesn't last more than half a day, but I do use it a lot for internet, videos and games so I'm used to charging it all night and at work. The processors are really fast and you now have a lot of memory. Enough to have multiple big programs open without issue. I used to have some issues installing many apps in my S2 because of small internal memory (not everything could be moved to the SD). I haven't had to worry about that with the S4 yet. I also tested the screen mirroring feature with a nice Samsung tv and there was some lag but it was OK. When watching a video, you can just send the video instead of the screen and then there is no lag. I also considered the S5 but this one was $400 and the S5 was more than $600.Overall really happy with it. If I learn how to configure the camera so the shutter is faster or it compensates for slight hand movements I will never let go of this phone.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: I don't know why I waited so long to buy this phone. I kept my last phone for over 6 years (Motorola Q9C) and it had always been nearly worthless for doing anything online, although I could get my e-mail through it and do some limited surfing if I had enough time and patience. I had an unbelievably cheap service plan that I was reluctant to give up (couldn't upgrade the phone and keep the plan). But I finally threw in the towel, and now I'm asking myself why I waited so long. This phone does EVERYTHING.The one thing I was reluctant about was typing on a touchscreen, which I have difficulty with. But then I realized I don't even need to type, I can dictate e-mails and texts! And it works great! I'm using it with Straight Talk on AT&T's network and it's been flawless, with 4g LTE speeds (need the correct APN settings to get LTE). The battery life has been good (although I am not a heavy user), and on standby it's phenomenal, it seems to barely use any power at all. The sound quality is great, the screen is great, and as each day passes and I learn more and more about how to use this phone, the happier I get with it.Prior to buying this I had read quite a bit about the fake phones out there and was determined to not be fooled into getting one of those. I did a lot of research about what to look for, and everything I read about this phone indicated it was the real deal. But one never knows until they get it in their hands. After receiving it I carefully examined everything, then downloaded and ran the "Genuine Galaxy - Phone Info" app which confirmed that the phone is authentic. What a relief. I bought this from seller "Mango Wireless," fulfilled by Amazon. It was brand new, in Samsung packaging. The only thing that seemed to be missing from the box was a warranty card (there were manufacturer's pamphlets explaining the warranty - but no card). It was listed in the description as being "Imported," which for cameras means there is no USA warranty. Not sure if that means the same thing for phones, but it probably does. I could have gotten the add-on 2-year protection plan for an additional $69, but decided to go without that. I figured I could send it back to Amazon within 30 days if need be, but if it ran fine during that time it would be unlikely to go bad over the succeeding 11 months anyway (Samsung's usual warranty is 1 year). I did get an Otterbox Defender case for it to provide some extra accident protection. Some may find that case to be too bulky, but I love it. I've had the phone for 3 weeks now and it is working perfectly.One of my passions is long-distance self-supported bicycle touring, and in the past I have taken a netbook, phone, mp3 player, camera and GPS with me, which adds quite a bit of weight to my setup. All of that will now be replaced with just this phone - awesome! This will lower my riding weight by 5 or 6 pounds and reduce the volume of supplies I need to carry. I am going to test it out on a tour starting in one week and will update this review with how it all worked out.I couldn't be happier with this phone.//UPDATE 12/7/2014// - The phone is still flawless. I've had it for 5 months now and still love it. No issues at all. On the bike tour it worked great. I kept an online blog, updating it only with this phone. I also used it to watch movies at night in my tent - I had about 25 movies copied to the memory card. The movies played flawlessly and the sound was good enough to not need earphones, but I often used earphones anyway. A 90-minute movie used about 20% of the battery charge which I thought was pretty good. No regrets.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: This review will be less about the product itself (because we all know this is an amazing, powerful, fast, feature-packed flagship smartphone) and more about SIM Compatibility/Network. Because that's why we're looking at unlocked phones, right?I digress. In June, I bought the Straight Talk Samsung Galaxy SIII from WalMart for $459.99. I thought it an amazing purchase that I would not regret... I was so wrong. Little did I know it was a CDMA phone (locked to Straight Talk) and it ran on the SPRINT network. This meant little to me then, but it means the world to me now, because I now know that Sprint is the most awful, bogged-down, spotty, insert-derogatory-phrase-here network on this entire planet. I live in Hawaii, and Sprint's network in Hawaii is an absolute joke. I had no service at my own house OR my workplace that was right on the convergence line of two Sprint towers (Sprint can't switch towers very easily so just went into roaming). The closest Sprint tower to my house had a coverage radius of roughly 1 mile, and still does. And worst of all, it's a 3G-only network. Not a regular 3G network, either. I got EDGE speeds at best. I've never pulled more than 1Mbps on the up or down. Straight Talk's S3 was also very watered down, with a locked bootloader and very limited software accessibility. It was a sad life for me.I had had it. I was done with this pitiful excuse for a carrier. All my friends had either AT&T or Verizon, who both had full LTE buildouts here. They always pulled webpages and videos like lightning. Their coverage was broad and consistent. I figured it was time to pinch a few pennies. I had heard that Straight Talk had released an LTE-capable AT&T SIM Card, and I needed a phone for it to power. I had my heart pretty much set on the S4, but didn't know which one to get.This was the one.The product arrived brand-new and completely unopened. Shiny new S4. I put it in the wall, popped the SIM in, edited the APN settings ([...]) and BAM. The 4G LTE icon popped up in the status bar, as well as two consistent bars of service. My experience thus far has been wonderful - I've been pulling a pretty steady 15up/19down everywhere I've been thus far. I now have coverage in my home. I have yet to see the NO SERVICE icon. This may not be a problem for many of you as you may be already coming from AT&T's network - but it's a whole different world for me.TL;DR? This phone works perfectly with Straight Talk's AT&T Micro SIM card. There is no hassle and no fuss - I set it up entirely online in 10 minutes. If these qualifications meet your criteria, buy this sucker - you won't be disappointed.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: Let me tell you the truth about this phone, and also about what is being offered vs what you get. I work in the Telecom market so I know the details about this phone.The phone itself is great. The problem is the type of S4 you are gettingFirst thing, this phone is NOT unlocked, once you insert a SIM card different from ATT, it will tell you that the phone is locked and you need to insert the PIN code. So if you want to use a different SIM from other carrier-country, you need to find-buy the code from the internet, then the phone is fully unlocked and any SIM will work.Second thing I encounter when trying to use a T-Mobile SIM Card. The AWS bands of T-Mobile for 3G/4G HSPA+ service are closed so you need to look on the Internet to find a way to open it if you want to have coverage on those technologies.Phone connects to T-Mobile LTE only after I dialed a number !!!Bottom line you are not getting a fully operating unlockable phone that can be used anywhere.You have to play with the configuration and search-change its configuration to make it work as a fully unlocked phone to be used with any SIM Card worldwide.

Product: Samsung Galaxy S4 SGH-I337 Unlocked GSM Smartphone w/ 13 MP Camera - Red (No Warranty)
Review: A tad bigger than the Samsung Skyrocket, this phone is fast. It has neither crashed, nor shown any lag. The screen and back of the phone can get a bit hot to touch. I find this happens with most Android phones I have owned (i.e. Captivate, Skyrocket, S4), especially if you have the back light raised to maximum output for longer than several minutes.A word of caution: if you are a developer or like to root your devices, you should be aware that you need the MDB/MDL firmware versions of the phone in order to install a recovery. If you have MF3, firmware, you will not be able to install recovery and it will be trick to root the device. I believe "safe strap" is the answer but I do not know the details. Look into this issue before purchasing. Further, if you do purchase a device and it is rootable, your carrier will likely try to push an over the air update to your firmware changing it to MF3 or beyond. If you want to root and install a recovery, you will have to stop this process. I advise you also look into this.Update Nov 2013: I believe there is a way to root and install a recovery on S4s with MF3 or later firmware. One method is called "Safe Strap."
